issue_title: UTM zone exceptions error
issue_body:
By definition zones are left-closed, right-open intervals, e.g. zone 31: 0 <= latitude < 6.
In function latlon_to_zone_number:

    if 72 <= latitude <= 84 and longitude >= 0:
        if longitude <= 9:
            return 31
        elif longitude <= 21:
            return 33
        elif longitude <= 33:
            return 35
        elif longitude <= 42:
            return 37

For latitudes >=72, this results in:
zone 31: 0  <= longitude <= 9
zone 33: 9  <   longitude <= 21
zone 35: 21<   longitude <= 33
zone 37: 33<   longitude <= 42

but for latitudes < 72:
zone 37: 36 <= latitude < 42

issue_title: Barrel vs Barrel
issue_body:
I noticed that barrel is using US Oil Barrel size of 42 US gallons. It's probably more common to convert barrels to other units when talking about beer, e.g. # of US pints in a 1/2 bbl (US beer barrel). 

It might be worth splitting barrel into:
  us_oil_barrel = 42 * gallon
  us_liquid_barrel = 31.5 * gallon
  us_beer_barrel = 31 * gallon
  imperial_barrel = 36 * imperial_gallon

See:
  https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barrel_(unit)

issue_title: Port sns sample to new kappa
attach additional storage during aegea launch
issue_body:
Again, apologies if I don't see this in the options. I built AMIs without configuring additional storage but most users are going to need an additional volume to download and work with their data. When using `aegea batch` it is straightforward to request `/mnt=500` or something but I don't see a way to do this with `aegea launch`. This would be a useful feature, as the alternatives are

a) rebuild the images so they have storage volumes already (which requires assumptions about space requirements), or
b) have people launch instances from the console, although they'd have to format the volume themselves.

issue_title: does fhog need padding?
issue_body:
Hello

I want to use dlib to build (using two students) an application which checks the presence and orientation of THT components mounted on printed circuit boards (capacitors, connectors IC's ...).
I consider that fhog classifiers should do the job (one for each type of component). For checking the orientation, I considered creating a tool like imglab, which also marks the orientation (4 possible directions). For training I considered chipping out all the positive images, rotate them to have the same orientation, and provide them to the trainer. To also have negative samples, the original image is also given to the trainer, with all the positive objects marked as ignore.
But after some experiments, the students are telling me that the detector trained with the original train_object_detector, by manually cropping and rotating the image chips, is not working. But it works if the components are marked as positive in the image of the entire PCB only when they have correct orientation, by rotating the entire image to get all components.
So, does the trainer need some padding around the positive samples?
If yes, how much is enough?

issue_title: Images from doctests should be opt-in, not opt-out
issue_body:
[Currently][1] it's necessary to set `NO_IMAGES=True` (or any value) for running doctests. This should be the opposite (i.e. `nox -s docs_images` should have to set an environment variable).

(It's also worth looking into `pytest` support for doctests.)

[1]: https://github.com/dhermes/bezier/blob/eaec7cfbba84cfb27247647be52286551df9e619/nox.py#L199

Hi,

Some package builders, as the Debian package builder (called buildd) using a chrooted environment, provide a $HOME=/nonexistant during the build.

You can try it  using the following command in a shell:
HOME=/nonexistant kde4-config --install include

In that case, the command provides the following output :

trying to create local folder /nonexistant: Permission denied
/usr/include/

This output fails the kde_check() function in extensions.py so kde_kwallet.so is not built.

I guess the warning message is provided on stderr, so maybe only catching the output on stdout could solve this issue.

Carl Chenet

issue_title: Handling the default namespace in XML
issue_body:
PROV-XML deserializer **does not** handle the default namespace in provx files. For example:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<prov:document xmlns:prov="http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#">
    <prov:entity xmlns="http://example.org/0/" prov:id="e001"/>
</prov:document>
```
should be deserialized as:
```
document
    default <http://example.org/0/>
    entity(e001)
endDocument
```

issue_title: PyHCL does not accept lists whose last entry is followed by a comma
issue_body:
The following is valid HCL and is in fact enforced both by Hashicorp's HCL formatter and many Terraform extensions for popular editors (e.g. VS Code, vim):

```
ip_ranges = [
  "1.2.3.4/32",
  "5.6.7.8/32",
]
```

However when parsed by PyHCL it gives an "unexpected RIGHTBRACKET" error. It appears that PyHCL has been confused by the comma after the second element `"5.6.7.8/32"` in the above example.

The HCL formatter enforces this last comma whenever the elements are placed on separate lines. As a workaround it is possible to put the elements on a single line; however, this is awkward for long lists or those where comments are required.

For compatibility, PyHCL should accept this trailing comma.

issue_title: Obscure XSS using url tag
issue_body: Hey there, today a few fellow students (@qll, @Immortalem, myself) took a look at your library and scanned it for potential security issues. We came up pretty much empty handed but were able to find a pretty nasty way to inject javascript through your parser, though not all browsers are vulnerable to this (most notably IE 10 and Chrome 40). The exact details of this XSS include sending, e.g. a `\x01` byte before the `javascript:` part so it bypasses your filter but still executes as valid JavaScript: Test We developed this case (and found other possible cases) using Shazzer (see [1] (http://shazzer.co.uk/database/All/Characters-before-javascript-uri) and [2](http://shazzer.co.uk/database/All/Characters-after-javascript-uri)). While this seems like a weird edge case, there actually lies a real vulnerability as any user could generate this and run JS in the context of another user. And Chrome 40 is actually the current recent version. Your current approach using blacklisting has the unfortunate result that there may always come up some way to bypass it (for example, there exists an ancient `vbscript:` URI for IE6 or something like that, though these would be very ancient machines indeed...). However, using a whitelisting approach would involve building a whitelist of allowed tags and that can be infinite (think `irc://`, `steam://`, ...). We would like to contribute to this project by offering a fix. But before blindly sending a pull request, we want to get your input on this. Method 1: We develop some method that fixes the above bug and still keeps blacklisting intact. While we feel this should be possible in a reasonably secure way, using the blacklisting approach might turn some other, weirder way, to evade it. However, we think such a fix is possible. Method 2: We implement a whitelisting approach that allows augmenting allowed tags by users of your libary. I'd personally prefer the first method as the other one limits functionality. However, since this is your library, we want this to be your decision in the end. Please let me know how you stand on this issue and we can get started on a fix :+1:

issue_title: Issue with not updating find_prompt when you enter/exit enable, config mode
tbutils ExceptionInfo and TracebackInfo to_dict() result not JSON serializable
issue_body:
I wanted to use tbutils to serialize whatever exceptions thrown at me and store them in a DB but `to_dict()` result is not JSON serializable which is kind of weird.

When I looked it up it seems that it's because `line` field in each `CallPoint` object is not of a native `str` type (although it says it is in the [docs](http://boltons.readthedocs.io/en/latest/tbutils.html#boltons.tbutils.Callpoint)). It is of type `boltons.tbutils._DeferredLine`.

I wrote a JSON serializer that handles that, but if there is no special reason for that I suggest returning a native string. Or at least update the documentation.

self.yearly_vol is type 'float' instead of type 'numpy.float64' like self.monthly_vol and self.daily_vol so when you try to calculate self.yearly_sharpe you get a division by zero error.

I managed a workaround by calculating the yearly volatility like:

`self.yearly_vol = yr.std() / np.sqrt(1)`

similar to how you calculated the daily and monthly volatility

best,
Spencer

issue_title: Gracefully exiting a Python process running Bjoern
issue_body:
Hi,

I have a simple Python script wrapping a Bjoern web server around a WSGI app. In essence, it looks like this:

```
def signal_term_handler(signal, frame):
    logging.info('got SIGTERM')
    sys.exit(0)

def main():
    signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, signal_term_handler)
    logging.info(" Starting Bjoern with WSGI app at http://{}:{}/".format(HOST, PORT))
    bjoern.run(router, HOST, PORT)
```

The server works just fine. The problem is that when sending a SIGTERM signal (e.g. using `kill -15`), the signal handler doesn't get called and the process doesn't die. When sending SIGKILL, the process does die, without calling the signal handler (this is expected). 

How can I intercept SIGTERM?

issue_title: Attribute parsing does not handle escaped backslashes correctly
Add ability to read local tlds file rather than download
issue_body:
Hi.  Thanks for creating URLExtract.  It works well for me locally, but when I deploy a Flask app that uses URLExtract to AWS ElasticBeanstalk (kind of like a PaaS), I get an error.

The problem is that AWS ElasticBeanstalk implements good web server security practices, which includes making the home directory, and the deployed code's directory, read-only.  They will have done this to make it harder for a web app vulnerability to results in a file being written to disk on the web server.

However, this presents a problem for URLExtract - if it's unable to write to the module's `__file__ ` or `$HOME` then it throws an exception because it has nowhere to store the TLDS file.

To get around this, it'd be great if there was the option to be able package the TLDS file as part of the deployed app, and pass the full path to this file to URLExtract at initialisation.  If passed a file path like this URLExtract would use the file, and not try to download a version from iana.org.

This approach also has the added benefit of being more robust - your app is not dependent on being able to download a file from iana.org to work.  It also allows you to guarantee two environments are exactly the same - they will have the same TLDS file (great for testing).  It's also more secure - pulling down files from third-party sites at runtime is considered risky by some, in fact in some commercial environments this is simply not possible - production outbound internet access is blocked for security reasons.

issue_title: add some argument to filter out recent versions of browsers
issue_body:
Seems like some websites are not happy with outdated user agents, e.g.

```
curl -vk "https://www.jdsports.co.uk/" -H "User-Agent:  Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10_9_2) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/34.0.1910.0 Safari/537.36" -H "Accept-Language: en-US,en;q=0.8,pl;q=0.6" -H "Accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8" -H "Connection: keep-alive" --compressed > /dev/null
```

results in

```
> GET / HTTP/1.1
> Host: www.jdsports.co.uk
> Accept-Encoding: deflate, gzip
> User-Agent:  Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10_9_2) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/34.0.1910.0 Safari/537.36
> Accept-Language: en-US,en;q=0.8,pl;q=0.6
> Accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8
> Connection: keep-alive
> 
< HTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden
* Server AkamaiGHost is not blacklisted
```

This uses very old user agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10_9_2) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/34.0.1910.0 Safari/537.36.

If I "upgrade" my user agent to most recent chrome I get 200 OK:

```
curl -vk "https://www.jdsports.co.uk/" -H "User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/56.0.2924.87 Safari/537.36" -H "Accept-Language: en-US,en;q=0.8,pl;q=0.6" -H "Accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8" -H "Connection: keep-alive" --compressed > /dev/null
```

response is:

```
GET / HTTP/1.1
> Host: www.jdsports.co.uk
> Accept-Encoding: deflate, gzip
> User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/56.0.2924.87 Safari/537.36
> Accept-Language: en-US,en;q=0.8,pl;q=0.6
> Accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8
> Connection: keep-alive
> 
< HTTP/1.1 200 OK
< Content-Encoding: gzip
```

This is not first time I'm seeing behavior like this. There are also website that do user agent checks server side and show some irritating messages to users about necessity of upgrading browsers. They should do this with JavaScript, but sometimes they do it server side and return some messages.

My idea is to either: limit number of ua versions to most recent by default. Or just add some argument that will specify some range of version numbers to go back to, e.g. range(5) would return only 5 most recent versions of Chrome.

issue_title: Incorrect urljoin when getting couchdb user
issue_body:
It appears that replacing urljoin by urllib.parse.urljoin causes a problem when getting a user from couchdb. I have found that it is caused by urllib handling the colon in typical user id e.g. org.couchdb.user:username

```
>>> from urlparse import urljoin
>>> urljoin('http://example.com', 'org.couchdb.user:username')
'org.couchdb.user:username'
```

The same applies to python 3.x

This leads to 
```
requests.exceptions.InvalidSchema: No connection adapters were found for 'org.couchdb.user:username'
```

To me it looks like urllib problem as I would say a colon is an allowed character.

issue_title: Missing default_graph in sparql_protocol writer
issue_body:
When writing to Openlink Virtuoso (07.20.3217) with the current sparql_protocol writer, a 400 bad request is returned with the following error:

> Virtuoso 37000 Error SP031: SPARQL compiler: No plain default graph specified in the preamble, but it is needed for triple constructor in DELETE {...} without GRAPH {...}

Examples of the sparql_protocol feature a `default_graph` attribute:
```
store = surf.Store(reader = 'sparql_protocol',
                   endpoint = 'http://dbpedia.org/sparql',
                   default_graph = 'http://dbpedia.org')
```

However, in the code there seems no sign of usage of this attribute, which is needed for triple stores like Virtuoso.

I will create a pull request for the fix.

issue_title: Restructure directories (binaries, installer, libraries) to allow for git branching
issue_body:
Thanks to Kevin Lawrence for the idea. Pasting email contents below:

--------------------------------------------------------------------

I think I understand the problem though. During build setup you put all of your 
library/binary files in cef3/[os]/ setup/binaries. If you switch branches those 
binary files would cause issues. Maybe you just need to namespace things then?

Have a directory structure like this:

cef3/[os]/build/[branch]/

Where [branch] contains the directories:
32
    - binary
    - installer
    - library
64
    -binary
    -installer
    - library

Make it so the build directory never gets committed in the .gitignore. Maybe 
you could update your command script to create the above directory structure 
for a given name and copy all of the install files/demo files in to their 
appropriate places. Something like:

compile.bat -make test, which would create a directory cef3/[os]/build/test/ 
with the above directories. Then you could fill in those directories with the 
appropriate files automatically.

This would allow you to keep the directories across branches and keep from 
committing them to the repository.

* Blocking: #172

I have bumpversion 0.5.3 running on linux mint 17 (which uses ubuntu 14.04.3). Every time I run bumpversion I get this error:

Traceback (most recent call last):
    File "/home/hodur/work/test/bumpversiontest/env/bin/bumpversion", line 11, in <module>
      sys.exit(main())
  File "/home/hodur/work/test/bumpversiontest/env/lib/python3.4/site-packages/bumpversion/__init__.py", line 659, in main
    if vcs.is_usable():
  File "/home/hodur/work/test/bumpversiontest/env/lib/python3.4/site-packages/bumpversion/__init__.py", line 83, in is_usable
    stdout=subprocess.PIPE
  File "/usr/lib/python3.4/subprocess.py", line 537, in call
    with Popen(*popenargs, **kwargs) as p:
  File "/usr/lib/python3.4/subprocess.py", line 859, in __init__
    restore_signals, start_new_session)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.4/subprocess.py", line 1457, in _execute_child
    raise child_exception_type(errno_num, err_msg)
NotADirectoryError: [Errno 20] Not a directory

If I install mercurial everything works as expected. I noticed that you're checking whether [errno equals 2](https://github.com/peritus/bumpversion/blob/master/bumpversion/__init__.py#L87) when you expect to catch this exception for this specific case. Is that a typo or does either windows or osx result in an exception with errno 2?

issue_title: Unverified HTTPS request
issue_body: I'm getting multiple (four) InsecureRequestWarnings from urllib3 on using it with Python 2.7.6.

issue_title: urlencode bug in python2
issue_body:
python2 version of `urllib.urlencode` encode unicode value to `?` or raise UnicodeError 

can pies overrider this function with a encoding parameter.

issue_title: Fix slice : position
issue_body: A lot of places have `x[start: end]`, with a space after the `:`. This is because I switched to autopep8, and it got rid of one space but not the other.

issue_title: Index out of bounds
issue_body:
I've come across a crash when using `cpp-coveralls` (latest version) to get the `gcov` output, filter it using `lcov` and upload the `lcov` output to coveralls with `cpp-coveralls`.  The crash surfaces in `parse_lcov_file_info` of `coverage.py`, I've added some print statements for debugging:

```python
def parse_lcov_file_info(args, filepath, line_iter, line_coverage_re, file_end_string):
    """ Parse the file content in lcov info file
     """
     coverage = []
     lines_covered = []
     for line in line_iter:
         if line != "end_of_record":
             line_coverage_match = line_coverage_re.match(line)
             if line_coverage_match:
                 line_no = line_coverage_match.group(1)
                 cov_count = int(line_coverage_match.group(2))
                 if args.max_cov_count:
                     if cov_count > args.max_cov_count:
                         cov_count = args.max_cov_count + 1
                 lines_covered.append((line_no, cov_count))
         else:
             break
 
     line_list = [line.rstrip('\n') for line in open(filepath, 'r')]
 
     num_code_lines = len(line_list)
 
     coverage = [None] * num_code_lines
 
     for line_covered in lines_covered:
         if int(line_covered[0]) - 1 < num_code_lines:
            coverage[int(line_covered[0]) - 1] = line_covered[1]
         else:
             
             print('an error occurred\n')
             print('num_code_lines', num_code_lines)
             print('line_covered', line_covered)
             print('line_list', line_list)
             print('coverage', coverage, '\n')
     
     return coverage
```
and for the `else` branch I get (for example)

issue_title: Support `--start-from-head` option
issue_body:
Allow getting just the latest log events by supporting `--start-from-head` option. By default (without that option), we would start getting only very latest log records ("tailing")

This is not so simple as it sounds (conflicting options, multiple scenarios...)

Implementation
=============

Using missing `startFromHead` parameter in `filter_log_events`
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[CloudWatchLogs.Client.get_log_events](http://boto3.readthedocs.org/en/latest/reference/services/logs.html#CloudWatchLogs.Client.get_log_events) supports parameter `startFromHead`

Unfortunately, [in code](https://github.com/jorgebastida/awslogs/blob/master/awslogs/core.py#L140) we use [CloudWatchLogs.Client.get_log_events](http://boto3.readthedocs.org/en/latest/reference/services/logs.html#CloudWatchLogs.Client.get_log_events) which does not support such option.

I would propose to keep using `get_log_events` method and use `startTime` with current time

Client having not synced clock
-------------------------------------------
Using current time can fail, if use would have bad time synchronization (being ahead of time would cause long period of silence).

Options are:

- ignore such scenario, declare time being properly synced as prerequisite
- get current time from some "live" request to AWS
- make one initial call to `get_log_events` with `startFromHead` being `False` and use timestamp from latest record as time for our `--start`. (did not test it if it returns any record at all if there is no new record added at that moment).
- after we do the call to `filter_log_events`, get the current time from HTTP headers of that request (I am not aware of simple method to do so) and if it would differ more than 30 seconds from current one, print warning to stderr and continue.

Ignoring seems fine to me.

Conflicting `--start` and `--start-from-head` options
-----------------------------------------------------------------------
These two options are mutually exclusive. In case they are both set by user, we have following options:

- use `--start` and ignore `--start-from-head`, possibly printing some warning to stderr
- reject such combination of options and exit

Exiting seems better to me (we will force user to think, what he really wants to get).

`--start-from-head` being off by default
-------------------------------------------------------
When there is no `--start` value, `awslogs` shall start from tail of log. This seems to me intuitive behaviour (user wanting to see, what is happening just now). This fits very well with `--watch`, where in many cases large logs would take too long time to show current records, which user probably cares about.

PS: Did I tell you I like the tool a lot?

namespace Foo {

issue_title: In and Contains are duplicates
issue_body:
The `In` and `Contains` validators are basically exactly the same. `In` came first (present in and before https://github.com/alecthomas/voluptuous/commit/fafc7204d7121645fc0fa18dc9df521431d000be 10 months ago, where as `Contain` was introduced 1 month ago in https://github.com/alecthomas/voluptuous/commit/b23fe91a04131ac9758aade47bd33706177f6477.

I suggest `Contains` is removed - that would break any projects that depend on voluptuous but the upgraded path is very simple so I don't think that's such a big deal.

What do you think?

issue_body:
Current issues are [documented](https://python3-krakenex.readthedocs.io/en/v0.1.4/#krakenex.API).

Can probably "just use `requests`", and offload any issues as "Not My Department".

Does require answering:

- [ ] Is having a dependency acceptable? So far, the package has had no dependencies other than the "standard library". `requests` is ubiquitous, though.
- [ ] Should the return value/format of `query_{private,public}()` remain the same?
- [ ] In either case, can a "unified" `query()` be introduced? What should its return value/format be?
- [ ] Should manual connection handling remain possible, or become hidden?

Answers may depend on current/planned use cases.

issue_title: __slots__ should be present by default, and other name for corresponding kwarg
issue_body:
Currently ``__slots__`` are not set by default (``slots=False``). I have two points here:
* This is not what user would expect, since dataclasses will be probably used for something that is present in large quantities, so that they should be lightweight. Also in other languages analogs of dataclasses are typically not extendable after creation.
* I would rather choose a different name for the corresponding kwarg, some people may not be familiar with slots and how they work. Maybe something like ``extendable`` that is ``False``?

issue_title: capture_start() does not immediately return
issue_body:
capture_start() runs _cmd() which waits for an ACK

I guess we could consider a parameter to _cmd that specifies if it should wait for ACK or not, or we can just build the _send right into capture_start(), but that's kinda hacky.

issue_title: Progress not working
Support standard kwargs to Extension
issue_body:
Currently, cppimport seems to rename certain keyword in cfg before passing them to setuptools.Extension, e.g. "compiler_args" -> "extra_compile_args" (https://github.com/tbenthompson/cppimport/blob/c187236363b8ad7df3b5be5186e204d03c965db6/cppimport/build_module.py#L124; I tried using extra_compile_args, with no success).  It would be appreciated if standard kwargs to setuptools.Extension were supported (instead?).  While the standard kwargs may or may not be very well named, they are, well, standard, and I think it's clearer to keep using them rather than introducing your own set of synonyms.

Thanks for the greatly useful project!

Support openssh match directives
issue_body:
Something like this
```
---
Matches:
    ? >-
      host
      10.40.220.16,
      10.40.220.39,
      !10.40.220.61,
      10.40.220.6?
    : # yes, have to tr ', ' to just ',' somewhere to support this construct
      inherits: user-ec2-template

    "host 10.40.184.30":
      identityfile: ~/.ssh/key2

    "host 10.40.223.19?,10.40.223.10?":
      user: ec2-user
      identityfile: ~/.ssh/key4

    "user appowner":
      identityfile: ~/.ssh/key5

# globs
    "host 10.40.22?.*":
      user: ubuntu
      identityfile: ~/.ssh/key2

Templates:
    user-ec2-template:    
      user: ec2-user
      identityfile: ~/.ssh/key1
```
Produces:

```
Match host 10.40.220.16,10.40.220.39,!10.40.220.61,10.40.220.6?
    user ec2-user
    identityfile ~/.ssh/key1

Match host 10.40.184.30
    identityfile ~/.ssh/key2

Match host 10.40.223.19?,10.40.223.10?
    user ec2-user
    identityfile ~/.ssh/key4

Match user appowner
    identityfile ~/.ssh/key5

Match host target_host exec not_inside_network
    ProxyCommand ssh -W %h:%p proxy_server

Match host 10.40.22?.*
    user ubuntu
    identityfile ~/.ssh/key2
```

issue_title: Is pyroaring safe for Gunicorn?
issue_body:
I'm using `pyroaring` with `Gunicorn` in `preload` mode, in which `Gunicorn` "load application code before the worker processes are forked".
I intend to load many `pyroaring` bitmaps and other big data structures in the *master* process and reuse them in multiple worker processes.
But something weird happens, the bitmaps in the worker processes seem to be polluted and each worker process return different kind of random results.
When `Gunicorn` restart worker processes, they start to return valid results again.
The only operations I use is `&` and `|`, so I believe it should be read-only.

issue_title: A conflict in validation between forgot_password view function and ForgotPasswordForm.validate method.
issue_body:
Using Flask_security `forgot_password` default view and turning ON the `SECURITY_CONFIRMABLE` flag, during validation process of the `ForgotPasswordForm`, the `forgot_password` view requires anonymous_user while the form checks if a confirmation is required which passes `self.user` to `requires_confirmation`. `self.user` is by default None and I don't see in related code how it could be something else, thus I got the following error each time I try to reset the password:
```
    File ".../lib/python3.4/site-packages/flask_security/confirmable.py", line 63, in requires_confirmation

    user.confirmed_at is None)

    AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'confirmed_at'
```

May be to solve this issue, before checking if a confirmation is requested, the form fetches the user using the entered email and thus `self.user` will not be `None`? Or am I missing something else here?

issue_title: Bookmarks in documents - propose they are span tags instead of anchors
issue_body:
If I have a simple docx with a link to a bookmark also inside that document, in word, clicking on the link goes to the text in the document.

Upon conversion, the location of the bookmark in the document is converted to another anchor tag, and the explicit link to the bookmark is the same tag. It doesn't have the same semantics as the original document. Was this intentional?

I propose that the actual bookmark itself is converted to a `span` tag, with an attribute of "data-mammoth-style":"bookmark" attached so that these elements can be found effectively if necessary. In the converted HTML, clicking on the link will go to the location of the span tag, like the original document.

issue_title: Double-nested query returns too few results
issue_body:
This somewhat complicated query returns the wrong results from Postgres. I compared with the exact same query through postgres' command-line tool. It would seem that one or both of the inner queries get limited, even though I never set the SqlMagic.autolimit setting.

```
SELECT country, month, avg(days) AS avg_days, avg(ratio) AS avg_per_active_day
FROM
    (SELECT country, month, device_uuid, count(day) AS days, sum(s) AS opens, sum(s)::FLOAT / count(day) AS ratio
    FROM
        (SELECT 
            country,
            date_trunc('month', date)::DATE AS month,
            date_trunc('day', date)::DATE AS day,
            event_daily_counts.device_uuid,
            sum(count) AS s
        FROM event_daily_counts 
        WHERE date >= '2016-01-01' AND DATE <= '2016-12-31' 
        GROUP BY 1, 2, 3, 4
        ORDER BY 1, 2, 3, 4) AS t
    GROUP BY country, month, device_uuid) AS t2
GROUP BY country, month
ORDER BY country, month
```

issue_title: Very minor issue: (lack of) compatibility with Micosoft ODBC Driver 11 for SQL Server for Linux
issue_body: Re: "Compatible with SQL Server , SQL Server Native Client , Micosoft ODBC Driver 11 for SQL Server and FreeTDS ODBC drivers" on the README. I've tested a fair amount of drivers against SQL Server 2008 through 2014 with Django. Since Django 1.7, the Micosoft ODBC Driver 11 for SQL Server has been problematic due to multithreading features introduced in Django 1.7. To get runserver to come up, for example, I had to use the following option: ./manage.py runserver --nothreading Additionally, other issues have cropped up, primary with "SQLRowCount returns 0" and SQLParam compatibility. More details here: http://stackoverflow.com/questions/28860521/large-select-query-hangs-using-python-and-unixodbc-connecting-to-sql-server I've switched back to FreeTDS which is working like a charm. We may want to remove the language about supporting the MS ODBC driver on the README unless a solution has been found (I'm happy to issue a PR for the minor change). Thanks for the great work, everyone.

```patch
diff --git netutils_linux_monitoring/base_top.py netutils_linux_monitoring/base_top.py

* no classifiers have been defined
* typo: extra_require should be extras_require (see the setuptools documentation [here](https://pythonhosted.org/setuptools/setuptools.html#declaring-extras-optional-features-with-their-own-dependencies))

I suggest fixing these issues and running the [pyroma](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/pyroma/) tool to check if it's fine.

issue_title: Detect CR/LF Issue
issue_body:
I was having a problem on Pypi with my README.rst where it was being truncated after the first line. A few Google searches later and I found this linter - which I appreciate since pypi is very picky and silent. Unfortunately, rst-lint returned a clean bill of health. I checked the file settings and it appears that PyCharm set the file to CR/LF line endings - which shouldn't choke Pypi but apparently did. Here is the project:

http://pypi.python.org/pypi/essentialdb/

I changed the file to just LF and Pypi was happy again. You can look back at versions 0.3.0 - 0.3.19 to see the issue. Might want to add that as a warning at least?

issue_title: Default allow_bulk_destroy implementation does the opposite of its documentation
issue_body:
class FooView(BulkDestroyAPIView):
    def allow_bulk_destroy(self, qs, filtered):
        # custom logic here

        # default checks if the qs was filtered
        # qs comes from self.get_queryset()
        # filtered comes from self.filter_queryset(qs)
        return qs is not filtered

By default it checks if the queryset was filtered and if not will not allow the bulk delete to complete.
 
I understood this to mean bulk_destroy will only apply to filtered querysets. However, the default implementation returns true when the queryset is not filtered and false when it is.

Then BulkDestroyModelMixin logic only allows unfiltered querysets to be destroyed.

Is this correct and intentional?

issue_title: Caffe converter shortcomings with Crop, Eltwise, Slice layers
issue_body:
I am converting network models from Caffe to MXNet using the `convert_symbol.py` conversion tool. The tool fails to properly convert several types of layers:

1) Caffe Crop layer: the converter ignores the "offset" parameters, and sets "center_crop=TRUE". Caffe crop does not do crop-to-center, even when no offset parameters are specified. Rather, Caffe defaults to offset values of zero.

2) Caffe Eltwise layer: the converter ignores the "operation" parameter, and converts any Eltwise layer to `mx.symbol.broadcast_add`, even though the Caffe operation may be `PROD` or `MAX`.

3) Caffe Slice layer: unsupported by the converter.

issue_title: NDArray::WaitToWrite() hangs in custom c++ operator. Bug?
example problem: where is `train_model`?
Embedding(sparse_grad=True) + hybridization fallback issues
issue_body:
With the mxnet master branch below code will fail. The combination of the following causes the failure:

- Use of Embedding(sparse_grad=True) + other operations in one HybridBloc
- Combining multiple forward passes during a single autograd record session

```import mxnet as mx


class Embedding(mx.gluon.HybridBlock):
    def __init__(self, num_tokens, embedding_size):
        super().__init__()
        self.num_tokens = num_tokens

        with self.name_scope():
            self.embedding = mx.gluon.nn.Embedding(
                num_tokens, embedding_size, sparse_grad=True)

    def hybrid_forward(self, F, words):
        emb = self.embedding(words)
        return emb + F.ones_like(emb)


ctx = mx.cpu()
embedding = Embedding(1000, 300)
embedding.initialize(ctx=ctx)
embedding.hybridize()

loss_function = mx.gluon.loss.SigmoidBinaryCrossEntropyLoss()
with mx.autograd.record():
    emb_in = embedding(mx.nd.arange(10, ctx=ctx))
    emb_in_2 = embedding(mx.nd.arange(10, ctx=ctx))
    loss = emb_in.sum() + emb_in_2.sum()
loss.backward()

print(embedding.embedding.weight.grad().data)
```

issue_title: Binary fails to run because of missing libpcre.so.1 on Ubuntu 16.04
issue_body:
Sorry if this is a duplicate, I couldn't find pcre mentioned in another issue. I tested this on v4.9.0 and v4.7.0.

Checking out the repo from source and building will result in a binary that fails because it is dynamically linked against libpcre.so.1. If I could install it then it would work just fine but it doesn't appear to be available in any of the stock repos (determined by running `apt-file find libpcre.so.1`). However, there are more recent versions available; libpcre.so.3 for example.

I was able to get it working by configuring it to explicitly use the later version.

```
sudo apt-get install libpcre3-dev
git clone https://github.com/facebook/watchman.git
cd watchman
./autogen.sh
./configure --with-pcre=/usr/bin/pcre-config
make
```

Shouldn't configure be able to determine that pcre isn't available on the system and/or find a valid version that is installed? Assuming my box isn't just weird, source builds should fail to run on 16.04 in general otherwise.

issue_title: "api_version" param is getting lost between adobject.Object.api_method() and api.call()
issue_body:
I am trying to make a single v2.5 call, I was hoping that it would be as easy as:

    fb_account = objects.AdAccount('act_123')
    fb_account.get_generate_previews(params={...}, api_version='v2.5')

`api_version` has no effect here as it's not being passed to `FacebookRequest`:
https://github.com/facebook/facebook-python-ads-sdk/blob/master/facebookads/adobjects/adaccount.py#L1358

Ok lets go one level deeper and try to manually create FacebookRequest:

    from facebookads.adobjects.adpreview import AdPreview
    fbrequest = FacebookRequest(
        node_id='act_123',
        method='GET',
        endpoint='/generatepreviews',
        api_version='v2.5',
        target_class=AdPreview,
        api_type='EDGE',
        response_parser=ObjectParser(target_class=AdPreview),

    fbrequest.execute()

This is still not working as it is probably getting lost when creating Cursor:

This is not especially a problem from the SDK but a problem for the API.
The call to ``https://graph.facebook.com/<API_VERSION>/act_<AD_ACCOUNT_ID>/adlabels`` with ``summary=1`` as a parameter is not return the total_count allowing us to get the ``total()`` from the EdgeIterator.
There is no reasons to not return the ``_total_count`` of this iterator because the easiest solution to find the number of objects of the account is to do ``get_ad_object(params={'limit': 1}).total()`` and for the adlabel we would have to do ``len(get_ad_labels(params={'limit': 10000}))`` which we really don't want to...

issue_title: Provide a way to filter out (or easily spot) messages triggered by jQuery UI or jQuery Mobile
issue_body:
Use case: I am using jQuery + jQueryUI + my own code depending on both, and I want to migrate to jQuery 3.

So, I'll use the jQuery Migrate plugin and look at the messages it generates pointing out migration issues.

Result: I am flooded by tons of warnings caused by jQuery UI using deprecated features. JqueryUI won't get rid of them and for a good reason, because they have to maintain bc with older jQuery versions.

Right now, the only way to distinguish between warning issued by jQuery Migrate that are triggered by my code, and hence need my attention, and those triggered by jQuery UI, which I have to ignore, is to inspect each trace one by one. That is painful and unviable.

Since there are official jQuery products out there (jQueryUI and probably jQueryMobile) that are and will keep using deprecated jQuery features, jQuery Migrate should provide a way to easily filter out or "spot" messages triggered by those product. It should recognize when a warning is caused by jQuery UI or jQuery Mobile, and there should be an option (probably enabled by default) to filter out these messages completely. Or, as a second-best alternative (actually, this would be desirable regardless of the former), the name of the file that has triggered the message should be included in the message itself instead of being buried in the trace. So, just by looking at the list of migration warning without inspecting them, you could more easily spot the ones that need attention.

issue_title: Rework clients CLI flags
issue_body:
Let's use non positionnal arguments for all our current CLI tools.

Python and NodeJS already depends on argparse, so we can easily just add `--` to them.

Only C++ would require more love, but we can likely find a library compatible with argparse, so we get consistent accross all and avoid the nasty effects we had to tackle.

issue_title: Crop GIF's black bars
Improvements to verify the token through the jwks
issue_body:
Currently, the only way to verify a token using the RS256 algorithm is to hard-code the signing key in a settings parameter.

I'm not really an expert here, but I have seen other implementations (not in Python) that take advantage of the (possible) JWKS endpoint of the identity provider. Documentation can be found here: https://auth0.com/docs/jwks.

I have a local branch of mozilla-django-oidc where it's working. Basically I verify whether a signing key or a JWKS endpoint is set, and in the latter case I try to match a key with the key id retrieved from the token and pass it on to jose to run the verification in place of the hard-coded one.
It works like a charm, except the fact that I have to decode the token first and to do that I have to use a private python-jose method (`jose.jws._load`) which I think is blatantly taken from pyjwt.

I have seen that a new branch has been made that uses the new josepy package, and it seems that decoding the token using that is no longer a private affair.

Can a modification like that be of any interest? It seems strange to me that I'm the only one needing  such a workflow.

class Root(luigi.WrapperTask):
    def requires(self):

class A(luigi.Task):
    def complete(self):
        return False

    def run(self):
        raise Exception()
```

issue_title: Compiler warning for test/unit/utRemoveComponent.cpp
CMake configure file does not return absolute paths
issue_body:
The problem with storing just the library name in `ASSIMP_LIBRARIES` and its location in `ASSIMP_LIBRARY_DIRS` is that `link_directories()` has to be called before creating the target that uses Assimp. CMake seems to be very confident that [the configure file should return absolute paths for libraries][1].

> Note that this command is rarely necessary. Library locations returned by find_package() and find_library() are absolute paths. Pass these absolute library file paths directly to the target_link_libraries() command. CMake will ensure the linker finds them.

[1]: http://www.cmake.org/cmake/help/v3.0/command/link_directories.html

issue_title: security group rule definition unclear
issue_body:
I'm trying to find documentation on the exact definition of a Security Group Rule. The AWS Service limits for an EC2-Classic SG say that the limit is 100 rules in the VPC User Guide:
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonVPC/latest/UserGuide/VPC_SecurityGroups.html#VPC_Security_Group_Differences

I have a rule that has '115 Permission Entries' under the **InBound Rules Count** column in the console. 

A majority of the rules as seen in the console are single public ip addresses to ports 80, 443, and 22. 
```
type protocol port-range        source
ssh    tcp           22          x.x.x.x/32
http   tcp           80          x.x.x.x/32
https  tcp          443          x.x.x.x/32
ssh    tcp           22          y.y.y.y/32
http   tcp           80          y.y.y.y/32
https  tcp          443          y.y.y.y/32
```
The Boto3 EC2 security group resource has an attribute `ip_permissions` which is  
> a list of one or more inbound rules associated with the security group.
 
http://boto3.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/services/ec2.html#EC2.SecurityGroup.ip_permissions

However, the number of inbound rules reports as being 3 rather than 6. 
```python
>>> import boto3
 
>>>ec2 = boto3.resource('ec2')
>>>security_group = ec2.SecurityGroup('sg-123456')
>>>len(security_group.ip_permissions)
3
```

the output of `security_group.ip_permissions` groups the rules by the `FromPort` key and aggregates the list of source ips in the `IpRanges` key

```python
>>>security_group.ip_permissions
[
  {
     ...
     u'FromPort': 22,
     u'IpRanges': [{u'CidrIp': 'x.x.x.x/32'}, {u'CidrIp': 'y.y.y.y/32'}]
     ...
  },
  {
     ...
     u'FromPort': 80,
     u'IpRanges': [{u'CidrIp': 'x.x.x.x/32'}, {u'CidrIp': 'y.y.y.y/32'}]
     ...
  },
  {
     ...
     u'FromPort': 443,
     u'IpRanges': [{u'CidrIp': 'x.x.x.x/32'}, {u'CidrIp': 'y.y.y.y/32'}]
     ...
  }
]
```


I'm assuming that the service limit must be evaluating the rules the same way the boto3 client reports and not the console since the security group still allows adding additional "rules" in the console, but I haven't been able to find any documentation that explicitly says that.

This seems to be contradictory to the security group definition found in the [Amazon EC2 Security Groups for Linux Instances](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/using-network-security.html#security-group-rules) doc:

> For each rule, you specify the following:
>* Protocol: The protocol to allow. The most common protocols are 6 (TCP) 17 (UDP), and 1 (ICMP).
>* Port range : For TCP, UDP, or a custom protocol, the range of ports to allow. You can specify a single 
>* port number (for example, 22), or range of port numbers (for example, 7000-8000).
>* ICMP type and code: For ICMP, the ICMP type and code.
>* Source or destination: The source (inbound rules) or destination (outbound rules) for the traffi

Are distinct rules only calculated based on the Protocol, Port Range, and ICMP Type? 


It seems that the boto3 client and the AWS console report different values.

issue_title: Change TranscribeService.Client.start_transcription_job() limit (10 jobs only)
issue_body:
Hi there,

many thanks for your `boto3` Pyton lib. I am working on auditio transcription ([odigo-auditor](https://github.com/korniichuk/odigo-auditor) open source lib) based on [Amazon Trinscribe](https://aws.amazon.com/transcribe/). How can I start more than 10  asynchronous job with  [TranscribeService.Client.start_transcription_job()](https://boto3.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/services/transcribe.html#TranscribeService.Client.start_transcription_job) boto3 function?
It is mean, that I run [TranscribeService.Client.start_transcription_job()](https://boto3.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/services/transcribe.html#TranscribeService.Client.start_transcription_job) function 123 times but [Amazon Trinscribe](https://aws.amazon.com/transcribe/) created only 10 first jobs.
Source code:
```python
#!/usr/bin/env python2
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

import urlparse
from os.path import basename, join

import boto3

bucket_name = 'transcribe.odigo-auditor'

def transcribe_dir(src, dst=None, language_code='en-US'):
    """Transcribe all mp3 file/files from directory (recursively) on AWS S3 with
       AWS Transcribe.
    Input:
        src -- S3 location of the dir (required | type: str). Example:
               'https://s3-eu-west-1.amazonaws.com/examplebucket/';
        dst -- S3 bucket where the transcription is stored (not required |
               type: str);
        language_code -- language code for the language used in the input mp3
                         file (not required | type: str | default: 'en-US').

    """

    while src.startswith('/'):
        src = src[1:]
    if not src.startswith('http'):
        bucket_name = src.split('/')[0]
        dir_name =  src.replace(bucket_name, '')
        while dir_name.startswith('/'):
            dir_name = dir_name[1:]
        location = boto3.client('s3') \
                .get_bucket_location(Bucket=bucket_name)['LocationConstraint']
        base_url = join('https://s3-%s.amazonaws.com' % location, bucket_name)
    else:
        path = urlparse.urlparse(src).path
        while path.startswith('/'):
            path = path[1:]
        bucket_name = path.split('/')[0]
        dir_name = path.replace(bucket_name, '')
        while dir_name.startswith('/'):
            dir_name = dir_name[1:]
        base_url = join('https://%s' % urlparse.urlparse(src).netloc,
                        bucket_name)
    for bucket in boto3.resource('s3').buckets.all():
        if bucket.name == bucket_name:
            for key in bucket.objects.all():
                if key.key.startswith(dir_name) and key.key.endswith('.mp3'):
                    url = join(base_url, key.key)
                    transcribe_mp3(url, dst)

def transcribe_mp3(src, dst=None, job_name=None, language_code='en-US'):
    """Transcribe mp3 file with AWS Transcribe.
    Input:
        src -- S3 location of the src mp3 file (required | type: str). Example:
               'https://s3-eu-west-1.amazonaws.com/examplebucket/example.mp3';
        dst -- S3 bucket where the transcription is stored (not required |
               type: str);
        job_name -- the name of the job (not required | type: str);
        language_code -- language code for the language used in the input mp3
                         file (not required | type: str | default: 'en-US').

    """

    transcribe = boto3.client('transcribe')
    if not job_name:
        job_name = basename(src).replace('.mp3', '')
    if not dst:
        try:
            response = transcribe.start_transcription_job(
                    TranscriptionJobName=job_name, Media={'MediaFileUri': src},
                    MediaFormat='mp3', LanguageCode=language_code,
                    Settings={'ShowSpeakerLabels': True, 'MaxSpeakerLabels': 2})
        except Exception as exception:
            return 1
    else:
        try:
            response = transcribe.start_transcription_job(
                    TranscriptionJobName=job_name, Media={'MediaFileUri': src},
                    MediaFormat='mp3', LanguageCode=language_code,
                    OutputBucketName = dst,
                    Settings={'ShowSpeakerLabels': True, 'MaxSpeakerLabels': 2})
        except Exception as exception:
            return 1
    return response

# Example. Transcribe
# 'https://s3-eu-west-1.amazonaws.com/examplebucket/example.mp3' file
#transcribe_mp3('https://s3-eu-west-1.amazonaws.com/examplebucket/example.mp3')

# Example. Transcribe
# 'https://s3-eu-west-1.amazonaws.com/examplebucket/example.mp3' file to
# 'examplebucket' bucket on AWS S3
#transcribe_mp3('https://s3-eu-west-1.amazonaws.com/examplebucket/example.mp3',
#               'examplebucket')

# Example. Transcribe all mp3 file from
# 'https://s3-eu-west-1.amazonaws.com/examplebucket/examples/' directory
# (recursively) on AWS S3 with AWS Transcribe
#transcribe_dir('https://s3-eu-west-1.amazonaws.com/examplebucket/examples/')
# or
#transcribe_dir('examplebucket/examples/')

# Example. Transcribe all mp3 file from
# 'https://s3-eu-west-1.amazonaws.com/examplebucket/examples/' directory
# (recursively) on AWS S3 to 'examplebucket' bucket on AWS S3
# with AWS Transcribe
#transcribe_dir('https://s3-eu-west-1.amazonaws.com/examplebucket/examples/',
#               'examplebucket')
# or
#transcribe_dir('examplebucket/examples/', 'examplebucket')
```

issue_title: Support encryption-at-rest for credentials
issue_body:
I'd like to propose adding functionality to support GPG encryption of credentials.

My initial thoughts are to implement this one of two ways:

1. If a `credentials.gpg` file exists use this in preference to the normal unencrypted `credentials` file.
2. Use a similar approach to that used in https://github.com/voxpupuli/hiera-eyaml which allows for the encrypted keys to be left in the credentials file - which improves readability whilst allowing keys to be kept securely.

In both approaches I would expect that most people would choose to use this in conjunction with `gpg-agent` which provides GPG credentials caching in a secure manner.

I would be happy to have a stab at the PR if the maintainers see this functionality as valuable.

issue_title: Botocore pagination params inconsistent
issue_body:
In the pagination interface, there are a few extra parameters related to pagination that are snake cased (``max_items``, ``page_size``, ``starting_token``). These are different than the camel casing of the parameters that are actually sent in the request. So you would get something like this (using ``s3.list_objects``):
```
paginator.paginate(Bucket='mybucket', page_size=1)
```

Ideally we would want to change the pagiantion related parameters to camel case as well (``MaxItems``, ``PageSize``, ``StartingToken``) for the sake of consistency. So the above pagination call would instead be:
```
paginator.paginate(Bucket='mybucket', PageSize=1)
```

Let me know what you think.

Hi, we have a backend using python 2.7.x, Im integrating Datadog APM following the documentation and I have the following error:
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/bottle.py", line 764, in _handle
    return route.call(**args)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/bottle.py", line 1575, in wrapper
    rv = callback(*a, **ka)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/ddtrace/contrib/bottle/trace.py", line 36, in wrapped
    resource = "%s %s" % (request.method, request.route.rule)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/bottle.py", line 1237, in __getattr__
    raise AttributeError('Attribute %r not defined.' % name)
```

when I go to trace.py line 33, I find this:
`resource = "%s %s" % (request.method, request.route.rule)`

somehow request.route doesn't exist, I think that probably is related to a different python or bottle version being used on our end.
Anyway `route` is already provided as a parameter for this method (`TracePlugin.apply(self, callback, route):`) so there's no need to call `request.route.rule`, you can call `route.rule` instead,
`resource = "%s %s" % (request.method, route.rule)`

Otherwise let me know if you have more details about this error and what's the right way to solve it,
thanks

issue_title: Open source s2protocol (+mpq parser) implementation in Java and Go
issue_body:
Hello Everyone,

I'd like to announce that I have implemented / ported the `s2protocol` library to **Java** and **Go** languages. They are available as open-source projects on Github.

Java implementation is part of the open source [Scelight](https://github.com/icza/scelight) project (which is much more than just a replay parser, it also gives a high-level api):

**Java MPQ parser:** https://github.com/icza/scelight/tree/master/src-app-libs/hu/belicza/andras/mpq
**Java s2protocol:** https://github.com/icza/scelight/tree/master/src-app/hu/scelight/sc2/rep/s2prot

Go implementation is a standalone project for both MPQ and s2protocol implementation:

**Go MPQ parser:** https://github.com/icza/mpq
**Go s2protocol:** https://github.com/icza/s2prot

The Go implementation is fairly new and will improve over time. As of May 22, 2017, s2prot is also available as a standalone, self-contained CLI app which parses a passed replay file, and outputs data from the replay in JSON format. The standalone s2prot app can be downloaded here: https://github.com/icza/s2prot/releases

Hope this helps others. Should you have any questions regarding these libraries, I'd be happy to answer them.

Have fun coding and parsing replays. Don't forget to star / watch if you like them.

issue_title: Failed import raises non-ImportError exception on MacOS
issue_body:
This is related to #657.

Apparently, MacOS adds a git executable that is just a stub which displays an error. This gets past the try/except I added in #658, and allows all of the GitPython components to be imported, but since the executable is not *actually* git, it results in an exception when ``refresh()`` attemepts to run a ``git version``:

```
  File "/opt/salt/lib/python2.7/site-packages/salt/utils/gitfs.py", line 90, in <module>
    import git
  File "/opt/salt/lib/python2.7/site-packages/git/__init__.py", line 82, in <module>
    refresh()
  File "/opt/salt/lib/python2.7/site-packages/git/__init__.py", line 73, in refresh
    if not Git.refresh(path=path):
  File "/opt/salt/lib/python2.7/site-packages/git/cmd.py", line 230, in refresh
    cls().version()
  File "/opt/salt/lib/python2.7/site-packages/git/cmd.py", line 551, in <lambda>
    return lambda *args, **kwargs: self._call_process(name, *args, **kwargs)
  File "/opt/salt/lib/python2.7/site-packages/git/cmd.py", line 1010, in _call_process
    return self.execute(call, **exec_kwargs)
  File "/opt/salt/lib/python2.7/site-packages/git/cmd.py", line 821, in execute
    raise GitCommandError(command, status, stderr_value, stdout_value)
GitCommandError: Cmd('git') failed due to: exit code(1)
  cmdline: git version
  stderr: 'xcode-select: error: no developer tools were found at '/Applications/Xcode.app', and no install could be requested (perhaps no UI is present), please install manually from 'developer
.apple.com'.'
```

Fix incoming.

`futures==3.1.1` gets installed and listed in `Pipfile.lock`.

`pipenv install azure-cosmosdb-table` in Python3.

</details>

##### Expected result

I expect to be able to install a pre-release like I would a full-release package if there are no full-releases for a package, or else to receive a note about the `--pre` flag.

##### Actual result

When I attempt to install a dependency currently in pre-release ([`black`](https://github.com/ambv/black) in the following instances) with `pipenv` and a `>=` pin, I'm told it's unable to resolve the dependency, and that the error exists in the subgraph. However, if I use a `==` pin, then everything works. This behavior is inconsistent and confusing, and the error message is misleading. I note that I can use the `>=` pin if I specify the `--pre` tag.

```console
$ # the following work
$ pipenv install black --pre
$ pipenv install 'black>=18.4a0' --pre # installs 18.4a2
$ pipenv install 'black==18.4a0'

$ # the following do not; error output below
$ pipenv install black
$ pipenv install 'black>=18.4a0'
```

The error message (shown in the output below) did nothing to make it clear that the `--pre` flag is missing from the last two commands. What's more, if the `--pre` flag is not required with `==`, then I would not expect it to be required with the `>=` pin.

Optimally, `pipenv` should have installed the package with a warning, or else asked me whether I wanted to install a pre-release, rather than error cryptically as it did. To clarify, if my options are all pre-releases, I think `pipenv` should succeed installation, either with a warning or after a prompt. At the very least, a better error message should be provided.

I imagine the `--pre` flag as being relevant only in the context of packages that have full-releases and pre-releases. My perspective is that if I have two choices for the latest, such as 1.3.0 and 2.0a1, I think `pipenv` should ask which I want. *However* I understand that this is a feature change and changes how `--pre` operates. Given the existing behavior but also that people new to `pipenv` are unaware of the `--pre` flag, it would be nice to print a message about the flag if an distribution like 2.0a1 is available but the `--pre` flag has not been specified.

Imagined behavior:

```console
$ pipenv install black # command should succeed ...
Warning! pipenv installed pre-release 18.4a2.

$ pipenv install black # ... or else ask for correct behavior
black does not have a full-release. Install pre-release 18.4a2? y
Installed black 18.4a2

$ pipenv install imagined-package # add warning text about --pre due to alpha version
Warning! imagined-package pre-release 2.0a1 is available. Stable version 1.3.0 was installed. Please use the --pre flag with pipenv if you meant to install version 2.0a1.

$ pipenv install imagined-package --pre # no changed behavior
Installed pre-release 2.0a1
```

##### Steps to replicate

Setup:

```console
$ python -V

issue_title: Since setuptools 28.5.0, a "global-exclude .pyc" no longer matches .pyc files
issue_body:
Since commit bb45468d27615c2ce9f9c9757a367c44d6ee80d2, if a project's MANIFEST.in contains ``global-exclude .py[co]``, the archive generated with ``python setup.py sdist`` will still contain all ``.pyc`` / ``.pyo`` files.

The test below shows that this line worked in setuptools 28.4.0, and broke in setuptools 28.5.0.

I'm not sure whether this can be considered a bug: the previous implementation looked up patterns without any anchoring, whereas the new one forces users to explicitly include wildcards in their ``MANIFEST.in``.
However, I think this change should have been mentioned in the version Changelog.

```patch
diff --cc setuptools/tests/test_manifest.py
index 6360270,ef4beda..0000000
--- a/setuptools/tests/test_manifest.py
+++ b/setuptools/tests/test_manifest.py
@@@ -425,12 -464,22 +425,19 @@@ class TestFileListTest(TempDirTestCase)
          assert file_list.files == ['b.txt']
          self.assertWarnings()
  
+         file_list = FileList()
+         file_list.files = ['a.py', 'b.txt', l('d/c.pyc'), 'e.pyo']
+         file_list.process_template_line('global-exclude .py[co]')
+         file_list.sort()
+         assert file_list.files == ['a.py', 'b.txt']
+         self.assertNoWarnings()
+ 
          # recursive-include
  ```

issue_title: Limit the amount of readable data from response body, honor content-length in response headers.
GridFS default chunkSize is no more 256k
issue_body:
[The param `chunkSize`](https://github.com/twisted/txmongo/blob/master/txmongo/_gridfs/grid_file.py#L29) is by default 256k in txmongo. However it is [no longer the default](https://docs.mongodb.org/manual/core/gridfs/) in MongoDB since 2.4.10:

> Changed in version 2.4.10: The default chunk size changed from 256 kB to 255 kB.

Reason invoked for this is "[to avoid overhead with usePowerOf2Sizes ](https://docs.mongodb.org/manual/release-notes/2.4-changelog/#id8)".

So I guess keep 256k as default in txmongo doesn't break anything, but it should be corrected anyway to get rid of this overhead and to stay consistent with default behavior.

I can do the PR if needed

issue_title: fetch_offsets unconditionally resets the offsets
issue_body:
Hi,

In pykafka v2.6.0, call to `fetch_offsets` will populate `partition_offsets_to_reset` and consumer will attempt to reset the offsets.
This is very unpleasant side-effect because we use pykafka to calculate consumer lag and except it to operate in read-only mode.

Is it possible to add some flag to make sure that `fetch_offsets` will not reset the offsets?

issue_title: RFC WebComment in Invenio 2
issue_body:
WebComment is the legacy commenting engine which allow users to add comments to records in the form of a discussion.

The current main features are:
* Replay to comment
* WYSIWYG/Markdown editor
* Filtering by text
* Filtering by file and version association
* Attach files to comments
* Associate comments to record files and versions
* Subscription to a comment
* Soft commenting deadline
* Custom extra headline for comments
* Collaps expand comments (persistent per user)
* Report abuse (+ admin tools)

It would be nice if the new implementation is more flexible allowing comments to be connected to a document (or anything) directly. 

One possible solution would be to build an extension that uses any of the existing commenting engines like [isso](https://github.com/posativ/isso/)(written in python) or [Disqus](https://disqus.com).

But I think we should also allow custom implementations as some of the functionalities we are using right now are quite hard to find.

cc @kasioumis

}
```
but it's correct request, while the response says: 400 (Bad Request). It would be nicer to have 404, error.

Also when you do  POST /register and provide non-unique username you get this:

```
400
{
  "username": [
    "This field must be unique."
  ]
}
```
however this is a business logic error and code 422 is better for such use case

**Generic message**

If I do POST /username and provide incorrect value of current_password I get this:

```
400
{
  "current_password": [
    "Invalid password."
  ]
}
```

Which makes it hard for the client (say Android mobile app) as I need to know and define maps ahead of time.

Would not it better to return something like this:

```
422
{
  "errors": 
      [
           {"field": "password", "message""Invalid password provided"},
           ....
     ]
}
```
the same would be for registration and other methods. That way I don't need to define the model for each JSON response body, as they're all generic. Otherwise for each API call you have to create a custom parser (and you have to know all the fields ahead of time). 

**Validation for Incorrect request**

If I do:
*PUT /me*
```
{
    "email": "admin@admin.com",
    "eeee": "jim",
    "zzz": "smith"
}
```

I'll get 200 HTTP code back:

```
{
  "email": "admin@admin.com",
  "id": 1,
  "username": "admin"
}
```
However the example above submitted 2 random fields. I would value if API return something like this:

```
422 or 400
{
  "errors": 
      [
           {"field": "eee", "message""Unknown field"},
           {"field": "zzz", "message""Unknown field"},
           ....
     ]
}
```

issue_title: evaluate() generates an extra reference for objects in calling frame
issue_body:
I find that `numexpr.evaluate()` generates an extra ref count for objects in calling frame, even when the object isn't used in the expression.  Here's an example:

```python
import numexpr
import numpy as np
import sys

def f():
    a = np.arange(10)
    numexpr.evaluate('1')
    print(sys.getrefcount(a))

def g():
    a = np.arange(10)
    print(sys.getrefcount(a))

f()
g()
```
```
3
2
```

This created a memory leak in my code: in my application, `a` was a big array that I was deleting, but it was never garbage collected because of the reference created by numexpr in a totally unrelated operation.

Notably, passing `local_dict={}` fixes the problem.  I think the usage of `sys._getframe().f_locals` is creating the persistent reference somehow, perhaps via a caching mechanism.  So this code also exhibits the leak:

```python
def h():
    a = np.arange(10)
    sys._getframe(0).f_locals
    print(sys.getrefcount(a))
h()
```

If I had to guess, I'd say this has to do with CPython fast locals (https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/master/Objects/frameobject.c), but I'm not enough of an expert to be able to tell for certain.

If one captures the f_locals dict then calls `clear()` on it, the leak disappears.  I have no idea if this is safe, though!

issue_title: Terminal content not re-scaling to plugin size
issue_body:
With the latest Qt 5.8.x and latest version of Spyder the terminal doesn't change its size to fit the window it is in. It throws this error:

    [...]
    spyder_terminal/widgets/terminalgui.py", line 50, in __init__
        self.view.page().contentsChanged.connect(self.contents_modified)
       AttributeError: 'WebPage' object has no attribute 'contentsChanged'

After chasing a bit the error through the code, the solution is pretty simple. From the [Qt documentation](http://doc.qt.io/qt-5/qwebenginepage.html) the signal from `QWebEnginePage`, `contentsChanged` does no longer exist but another one `contentsSizeChanged` does exist.

Line 50 of `terminalgui.py` has to be changed from

    self.view.page().contentsChanged.connect(self.contents_modified)

to:

    self.view.page().contentsSizeChanged.connect(self.contents_modified)

I'm not even sure if it is what it is suppose to do. But either commenting or changing the line above seems to help it rendering the terminal full size. If not it crashes and it can't load properly.

issue_title: CircleCI + KeenClient-Python
issue_body:
When installing a recent keen python client on CircleCI under python 2.7.9, I ran into an error.

 Circle's 2.7.9 virtualenv is using pip 1.4.1, which is truly ancient, but CircleCI is a pretty popular build environment.  I think an appropriate fix would either be to try/except in the setup.py around parse_requirements, or to document this specific error with the workaround suggestion of upgrading CircleCI's install pip.

I'll comment below with the original build error as well as the workaround that worked for me.

Alternatively, I think this would work around the problem in setup.py:

```
try: 
    install_reqs = parse_requirements('requirements.txt', session=pip.download.PipSession())
except AttributeError:
    # compatibility for pip < 1.5.6 (which CircleCI uses as of 20150401)
    install_reqs = parse_requirements('requirements.txt')
```

Icon Request: fa-icon-on-foot
issue_body:
Hi there,

Love the icon set - incredibly well designed and useful. I notice you welcome suggestions for new icons so thought I'd share my own suggestion as I found myself in need of a particular icon that I couldn't see in the current set. It's an icon for a person 'on foot' to compliment the existing transportation icon set. 

For example, if I was using the icons in the context of a travel experience I might wish to see options available to me by car, cycling, walking or public transport such as bus or trains. Most of these transport options are already covered but I wasn't able to find one to represent 'walking' - hence my suggestion. Another use case could be to denote 'nearby' in icon form. 

In terms of style, I'd propose a similarity to the current icon-set for transport, so the icon would sit alongside these in many contexts. There is a male/female icon which could be tweaked a little to represent movement (e.g. slightly bent leg to denote walking). Hope that's useful. Look forward to seeing further updates :-)

issue_title: Pre-compile yaml into py file
issue_body:
For context, see: https://github.com/getsentry/sentry/pull/1987

I notice this is being translated from yaml to json inside the sdist. Why not go one step further and generate the .py version instead?

tl;dr,

It takes 1.2 _seconds_ to import the module on my computer through parsing the yaml file and converting it at module import time, but 219 microseconds after it's compiled to a py file.

Before:
```
$ python -m timeit -s 'from sentry.utils.ua_parser import parser' 'reload(parser)'
10 loops, best of 3: 1.2 sec per loop
```

After:
```
$ python -m timeit -s 'from sentry.utils.ua_parser import parser' 'reload(parser)'
1000 loops, best of 3: 219 usec per loop
```

I can submit pull request for this if you're on board.

issue_title: Manticore adds an extra parameter to methods without them when it shows the resulting transactions
issue_body:
### OS / Environment

Distributor ID:	ArchLinux
Description:	ArchLinux
Release:	rolling-release

### Manticore version

Last revision (aa792588fe3a883276ea30ba543ec8822cf44db0)

### Python version

Python 2.7.14

### Summary of the problem

Manticore adds an extra parameter to methods without them when it shows the transactions.

### Step to reproduce the behavior

This is a small solidity contract to reproduce this issue:

```solidity
contract C {
  function f() {
    return;
  }
}
```

### Expected behavior

I expect to find transactions like this:

```
...

Transactions Nr. 1
Type: Call
From: 0xd51f25e60490392aa9eb72624f93de30ccd111f3 
To: 0x1bfa530d5d685155e98cd7d9dd23f7b6a801cfef 
Value: 1 (*)
Data:  (*)
Return_data:  

Function call:
f() -> REVERT (*)
```

### Actual behavior

I got transactions like this one: 

```
...

Transactions Nr. 1
Type: Call
From: 0xd51f25e60490392aa9eb72624f93de30ccd111f3 
To: 0x1bfa530d5d685155e98cd7d9dd23f7b6a801cfef 
Value: 1 (*)
Data:  (*)
Return_data:  

Function call:
f(240) -> REVERT (*)
```

issue_title: Antenna transitions not used in dealiasing routines
issue_body: The antenna_transition* attribute of the **Radar** object is not used in the dealiasing algorithms (or other algorithms) in Py-ART.  This means that rays in which the antenna is in transitions between sweeps are treated like collected sweeps in these algorithms. These ray should be ignored by default and not included in the correction.  One possible solution would be to add an `exclude_transition` method to the **Gatefilter** class and have all gates in rays which are marked as in transition be excluded by default by the `moment_based_gate_filter` function.

issue_title: Working around abysmal google.cloud.pubsub.Subscription.pull() delays?
issue_body: So I'm using psq in a light-demand environment, and am encountering abysmal latency on the workers pulling jobs off the queue. As in 10 minutes when things are "speedy", and up to 25 minutes in some cases. I see that the code depends on the behaviour of google.cloud.pubsub.Subscription.pull() in blocking mode. Would modifying the psq code to do its own polling on the unblocked pull() improve that, or would the underlying subscription service still be taking its own sweet time to deliver messages even if we were banging on it every 10 seconds? I can dig into this, but thought you might already have some insights on this problem.

issue_title: feature request: better api for arbitrary keyword argument pass to query ?
issue_body:
from this example https://googlecloudplatform.github.io/google-cloud-python/stable/bigquery-usage.html#querying-data-asynchronous

```python
>>> dataset = client.dataset('dataset_name')
>>> table = dataset.table(name='person_ages')
>>> job = client.run_async_query('fullname-age-query-job', query)
>>> job.destination = table
>>> job.write_disposition= 'WRITE_TRUNCATE'
>>> job.name
'fullname-age-query-job'
>>> job.job_type
'query'
>>> job.created
None
>>> job.state
None
```

how about a better api by allowing arbitrary keyword list to be appended on the insert job POST request
https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/reference/rest/v2/jobs the keyword `destinationTable` and `writeDisposition` here are from rest api reference, this way can keep `google-cloud-python` library to be a thinner layer, just gives user the best flexibility

```python
>>> job = client.run_async_query(query,
          destinationTable={'datasetId': 'dataset_name', 'tableId': 'person_ages'},
          writeDisposition='WRITE_TRUNCATE',
          )
```

in python it can be implemented as dict as last argument:

```python
def run_async_query(query, **kwargs):
    # populate from kwargs to be on the `configuration.query` in the post request body,
    # it could optionally have jobId
```

Btw, from #3209 I am not puting a `job_name` / `jobid` field because I don't think that's necessary, the `run_async_query` shouldn't require user to provide a `jobid`, in the rest api insert job request, if jobid is absent, server side would generate a unique id

issue_title: copy2() resets content-type to binary/octet-stream if metadata is set
issue_body:
A file copied with copy2 (is there a copy1?) resets the content-type to binary/octet-stream on the destination file if the file is copied. The documentation does not specify that the metadata should contain the content-type (or how it could contain a content-type), and content-type is also a separate parameter using open(), so the expectation is that the content-type should be copied with such a call:

    copy2(src, dst, {'x-goog-acl': 'public-read'})

Thank you!

issue_title: Feature Request: Bundle of TimeSeries
issue_body:
In #169 I outlined a use case for `Domains`. In writing up that usecase, I realized that I could also work with a "Bundle" TimeSeries. Namely, I often work with a number of `boolean` valued time series whose values are constant for large periods of time. However, combining the time series multiplies the number of points required to represent the time series (particularly if combined with a real valued signal).

My current solution has been to create a dictionary of `TimeSeries` objects, but this ran into problems when the timeseries are defined on different domains. It would be nice to have an object that "bundles" multiple `TimeSeries` objects but asserts that they all have the same domain.

issue_title: make error messages in concurrent work in jvm compile execution_graph.py respect --print-exception-stacktrace
issue_body: If an error occurs while processing the result of e.g. a zinc compile, the error message will be shown alone, without a corresponding exception type, line number, or stacktrace. If we add `log.debug(traceback.format_exc())` at line 267 of `jvm_compile`'s `execution_graph.py`, then [if a zinc job(s) fails](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/130a937e8d4dbb3084775b7c627acf3f4b31c398/src/python/pants/backend/jvm/tasks/jvm_compile/execution_graph.py#L326), we get to see the entire stacktrace as well. This makes errors that bubble up from invoking the `work()` method much easier to diagnose and fix.

issue_title: Fix developer thrash on the `native_engine_version` file
issue_body: Right now, we deploy the native engine code as a seperate binary and version the binary via a hash of its sources, the value being stored in `src/python/pants/engine/native_engine_version`. With multiple developers starting to hack on the native engine more regularly, this files contents conflict and the dev experience is suboptimal.

issue_title: Provide a configuration item like <dependencyManagement> in maven to pin transitive jar versions
issue_body:
In the Square repo, I've bent over backwards in some places to force jars to be updated to a certain version by manually adding dependencies to force a transitive dependency.  We already have a maven `<dependencyManagement>` section in our maven config, it would be nice to just tell ivy about it.  

Comments in ivy_util.py[1] s something about overrides being used just for transitive deps, which I hope means that it won't pull in the jar explicitly unless another dep needs it.  This sounds like a solution to get parity with Maven resolution, but currently, the ivy step only seems to include explicit dependencies listed in BUILD files.  Instead I would want to pass some list of M2coordinates with explicit versions, regardless of whether they are explicitly depended on.

John replied:
It does sound like <override/> does what you want, you just need to plumb the concept to a repo-wide config item - a list of global overrides - and/or a new JvmTarget attribute, right now it just works on force=True JarDependency objects in the transitive BUILD dep graph.

[1] https://rbcommons.com/s/twitter/r/2823/diff/1?file=2434329#file2434329line386
