issue_title: PrepCommand targets should accept multiple goals
issue_body:
Currently [only 1 goal](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/src/python/pants/build_graph/prep_command.py#L44) can be specified leading to copy/paste boilerplate when its desired to have the `PrepCommand` run prior to several goals.

For example, I'm having to do this over in Aurora:
```python
# build-support/thrift/BUILD
prep_command(
  name='prepare_binary_compile',
  goal='compile',
  prep_environ=False,
  prep_executable='build-support/thrift/prepare_binary.sh',
)

prep_command(
  name='prepare_binary_binary',
  goal='binary',
  prep_environ=False,
  prep_executable='build-support/thrift/prepare_binary.sh',
)

prep_command(
  name='prepare_binary_test',
  goal='test',
  prep_environ=False,
  prep_executable='build-support/thrift/prepare_binary.sh',
)

target(
  name='prepare_binary',
  dependencies=[
    ':prepare_binary_compile',
    ':prepare_binary_binary',
    ':prepare_binary_test',
  ],
)
```

And then add the `build-support/thrift:prepare_binary` to thrift targets.

issue_title: Debugging Sockeye Extensions
issue_body:
What is the best way to debug the mxnet symbolic API (e.g. tensor shapes) during development. 
One idea I have heard some people recommend is to swap out the entire symbolic API for the ndarray API, during debugging - is there a better way to go about this?

If this is a common use case, does it make sense to modify Sockeye so that swapping symbolic and imperative interfaces is relatively straightforward?

issue_title: Sample indexes are outdated
issue_body:
`$ ipwb replay ipwb/samples/indexes/sample-1.cdxj` starts fine reporting 65 mementos from bitchmagazine.org. Accessing one of the URIs in the replay system, however, simply shows "Error" in the viewport. The replay python code report the following:
```
looking for org,bitchmagazine)/tag/guns-in-school 20130411210714 in ipwb/samples/indexes/sample-1.cdxj
'locator'
127.0.0.1 - - [31/May/2017 13:13:57] "GET /20130411210714/bitchmagazine.org/tag/guns-in-school HTTP/1.1" 500 -
```
This may be due to its creation prior to our usage of the "locator" field. In the sample CDXJ files, the "locator" is split into two JSON fields, `payload_digest` and `header_digest`. The absence of a locator field causes ipwb replay to return an error.

issue_title: BinaryTokenSignature and UsernameToken create two Security headers
issue_body:
`BinaryTokenSignature` and `UsernameToken` create two Security headers and the service that I'm working with doesn't like that...

```
Internal Server Error: A web service security fault occurred[{http://docs.oasis-open.org/wss/2004/01/oasis-200401-wss-wssecurity-secext-1.0.xsd}InvalidSecurity][Found more than one Security header for role null]. Reference code:  82ac7756-fe7b-4d80-875f-0f30f1867d6a
```

I'm only helping someone else set up a SOAP client so I don't know SOAP very well at all, but the SOAPUI output combines the two into one Security header, and that works for us. I modified both classes to find and add to an existing Security header if one exists. This might not always be what's required, but I couldn't see where/how to specify roles for the Security header.

Pull request to come.

issue_title: BGP session tear down the session after several hours [3.4.16]
ExaBGP unconditionally sets TCP_MD5SIG sockopt on Linux.
issue_body:
<!---
Hello,

We are sorry that you are experiencing an issue with ExaBGP.

Before opening this issue could you please:
 - make sure the problem was not already reported to avoid duplicates.
 - check if the problem is still present on the latest version
   using the 3.4 or master branch (if you know what it means)
   alternatively the latest release (3.4 or 4.0)

For us to help you the outpout of exabgp -d is often required.
Please use ``` for any copied output.
-->

##### Summary
ExaBGP 4.0.6 unconditionally sets socket to TCP_MD5SIG, even if MD5 isn't in use.



##### OS
Linux 4.17.3 WITHOUT CONFIG_TCP_MD5SIG

```
Linux host1.example.com 4.17.3-36.el7 #1 SMP Fri Jun 29 19:15:06 UTC 2018 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

##### Version
<!--
Paste verbatim the output from “exabgp --version” between quotes below
-->
```
ExaBGP : 4.0.6-daa3d6ba
Python : 3.6.3 (default, Mar 20 2018, 13:50:41)  [GCC 4.8.5 20150623 (Red Hat 4.8.5-16)]
Uname  : Linux host.example.com 4.17.3-36.el7 #1 SMP Fri Jun 29 19:15:06 UTC 2018 x86_64
Root   : /opt/rh/rh-python36/root/usr
```

##### Installation
<!--
Please let us know how you installed ExaBGP 
-->
```
Custom RPM Package
```


##### Program output
<!-- 
When encountering a problem please run exabgp with the "-d" option

THIS IS THE MOST IMPORTANT INFORMATION - WITHOUT IT WE ARE UNLIKELY TO BE ABLE TO HELP
-->
```
17:44:37 | 410288 | outgoing-1    | outgoing-1 2620:xxxx:43:c103::5-2620:149:43:c103::1, closing connection
17:44:37 | 410288 | outgoing-1    | connection to 2620:xxxx:43:c103::1:179 failed, This linux machine does not support TCP_MD5SIG, you can not use MD5 ([Errno ENOPROTOOPT] [Errno 92] Protocol not available)
17:44:37 | 410288 | reactor       | initialising connection to peer-1
17:44:37 | 410288 | outgoing-1    | attempting connection to 2620:xxxx:43:c103::1:179
17:44:37 | 410288 | outgoing-1    | outgoing-1 2620:xxxx:43:c103::5-2620:xxxx:43:c103::1, closing connection
```
##### Importance
<!--
Please let us know if the issue is affecting you in a production environment
-->
```
Trying to upgrade 3.4.7
```

It appears that `lib/exabgp/reactor/network/tcp.py` used to wrap the whole MD5 function in `if md5` but that moved to line 129,  and doesn't wrap the `setsockopt()` anymore.

```python
if md5:
    # .. do stuff ..

# does this even if no md5 is configured (line 173)
io.setsockopt(socket.IPPROTO_TCP, TCP_MD5SIG, sockaddr + key)
```

issue_body:
There does not seem to be away to build both masters and instances from design space file at the same time, `fontmake -m Mada.designspace` will build the masters only, and `fontmake -i -m Mada.designspace` will build the instances only, but I’d like to build both in the same invocation.

issue_title: Cleaner workflow for Monotype feature files
issue_body:
For the Noto project, our main vendor (Monotype, MTI) delivers feature files in the [Monotype feature file format](http://monotype.github.io/OpenType_Table_Source/otl_source.html); see [Noto Sans Khmer](https://github.com/googlei18n/noto-source/tree/master/src/NotoSansKhmer) for an example. fonttools.mtiLib can compile these files, but the fontmake workflow is messy because this side input gets injected late in the pipeline.

To clean this up, I wonder if we could change fontmake so it produces self-contained UFOs for the interpolation masters. Other than today, those UFOs would contain everything needed for being compiled to binary OpenType files. Instead of the usual “features.fea” in Adobe format, these UFOs would contain MTI feature files such as “features_GPOS.mti” and “features_GSUB.mti”.

Compared to what fontmake does today, this would be only a very minor difference: the same files  in the same format as today, they just would get copied into the master UFOs early in the compilation process. Self-contained masters would make it easier for other tools (such as TruFont, or ad-hoc scripts) to process the Noto masters. Likewise for other fonts that get produced with a similar workflow.

Generating static UFO instances in this format would also be doable, but trickier (needs a decompiler). Personally, I care mostly about variable fonts, so I wouldn’t mind if this was only implemented for interpolation masters.

issue_title: Weight axis should use weightClass property
issue_body: According to section “Variation Axis Tags” in the [OpenType specification for “fvar”](https://www.microsoft.com/typography/otspec/fvar.htm), the values for the weight axis must correspond to [usWeightClass in “OS/2”](https://www.microsoft.com/typography/otspec/os2.htm#wtc). For example, Light must be at `wght=300`, Regular at `wght=400`, Black at `wght=900`. Currently, however, fontmake produces variable fonts whose weight axis is violating the OpenType spec: ```xml wght 26.0 90.0 190.0 ``` Our Glyphs sources already contain the needed information as a `weightClass` property: But fontmake currently seems to ignore this property. Instead, it should use the property so that the produced variable font has the correct (according to spec) range for weight. Also, the produced font should not contain an `xxxx` axis; filing a separate bug for that.

issue_title: provide subscription topic in event details for non-pattern-based subscriptions
issue_body:
Currently the subscription handler receives the event details as they come over the wire. For non-pattern-based subscriptions, the subscription topic is not sent over the wire since the client has the knowledge. Consequently the subscription details do not provide the topic.

This is unexpected behavior, since it exposes a wire-level optimization to the user.

In Autobahn|JS, the subscription topic is provided for all events. Autobahn|Python should have the same behavior.

issue_title: Figure out what's going on with port/vport in 2.7.2
issue_body:
Long story...

Last December, working on a10-openstack, we discovered that the "port" parameter for virtual servers was actually "vport".  We changed it and stuff appeared to work.

Over the last 11 months, we have gone back and forth fixing this bug and re-introducing it.  I think we need to create a generic library for 2.7.2/4.0 discrepancies that we can use across all projects so we can stop the see-saw that is this bug.

On master, if I run `trinity` in a fresh data dir, I get:

It appears that /tmp/trinity-ropsten/logs/trinity.log does not exist.
Trinity does not attempt to create directories outside of its root path
Either manually create the path or ensure you are using a data directory
inside the XDG_TRINITY_ROOT path

Ok, fair enough, so I create a logs directory and try again
```
$ mkdir -p /tmp/trinity-ropsten/logs
$ trinity --data-dir /tmp/trinity-ropsten/ --ropsten --light
   ERROR  06-11 13:23:38        main  
It appears that /tmp/trinity-ropsten/logs/trinity.log does not exist.
Trinity does not attempt to create directories outside of its root path
Either manually create the path or ensure you are using a data directory
inside the XDG_TRINITY_ROOT path

No dice. Maybe it means the file itself must be present?

```

    INFO  06-11 13:24:32     logging  Trinity DEBUG log file is created at /tmp/trinity-ropsten/logs/trinity.log

issue_title: h5py >=2.4.0 not detected by pip/setuptools
issue_body:
I'm trying to build a conda package which depends on ``h5py``. Here's a fragment from my ``meta.yaml`` recipe:

```
build:
  binary_relocation: true
  number: 0
  script: python setup.py install
package:
  name: xyz
  version: 0.2.1
requirements:
  build:
  - h5py 
  - python
  run:
  - h5py 
  - python
source:
  path: C:\source
test:
  requires: []
```

When I try to build this package, setuptools does not detect that ``h5py`` is already installed in the build environment and the build fails:

```
c:\> conda build c:\recipe
...
Processing dependencies for xyz==0.2.1
Searching for h5py

RuntimeError: Setuptools downloading is disabled in conda build. Be sure to add all dependencies in the meta.yaml  url=https://pypi.python.o
rg/simple/h5py/r
```

It works fine if I change the requirement to ``h5py <=2.3``. But since version 2.4 h5py can't be detected by pip or setuptools:

```
C:\>conda create -n bug pip
C:\>activate bug

[bug] C:\>conda install h5py=2.3
[bug] C:\>pip install h5py
Requirement already satisfied (use --upgrade to upgrade): h5py in c:\anaconda\envs\bug\lib\site-packages

[bug] C:\>conda install h5py=2.4
[bug] C:\>pip install h5py
Collecting h5py
  Using cached h5py-2.5.0.tar.gz
```

This might be the case because ``conda install h5py=2.3`` installs ``h5py-2.3.1-py2.7.egg-info`` in ``site-packages``, but this directory is missing if you install ``h5py >=2.4``.

issue_title: conda create requires admin access on Windows in a multiuser installation
issue_body:
We are running Minoconda on a server machine for multiple users.
Miniconda is installed in C:\Miniconda3.
I set the permissions of the C:\Miniconda3 folder (and subfolders) such that all users have write access. Also explicitly for C:\Miniconda3\envs.
When doing a conda create -n p35 anaconda python=3.5 (also tried with python=2 and python=3), the system asks for admin rights for the executable pythonw.exe. This happens at the end, when all packages are downloaded and some linking needs to be done.
So users cannot create their own environments (so I am assuming that some temporary files are created or other files are written to other locations.
Would it be possible to make it so that users can create their own environments without needing an administrator password?

issue_title: conda create --file does not understand pip file specifications
issue_body:
It would be nice to be able to install at least of a subset of pip requirements files via conda. Right now, as soon as the requirements file has an exact version specification, this does not work, as pip expects "==" while conda uses "=".

Is there a reason why conda could not also understand "==" for exact version specifications? This would make `conda create --file` compatible with a broader subset of pip requirements.txt files. (I am willing to work on a patch.)

issue_title: set_tags() can't handle byte-array auxiliary tags
issue_body:
This is originally from [biostars](https://www.biostars.org/p/236819/). If you try to `set_tags()` using a byte array then the following error is produced:

    Traceback (most recent call last):
      File "./foo.py", line 9, in <module>
        read.set_tags(tags)
      File "pysam/libcalignedsegment.pyx", line 2113, in pysam.libcalignedsegment.AlignedSegment.set_tags (pysam/libcalignedsegment.c:23066)
      File "pysam/libcalignedsegment.pyx", line 295, in pysam.libcalignedsegment.packTags (pysam/libcalignedsegment.c:6217)
    ValueError: invalid value type 'B' (<type 'str'>)

I'm attaching a very very small BAM file that can produce this error with the following code:

    #!/usr/bin/env python
    import pysam

    bam = pysam.AlignmentFile("small.bam")
    for read in bam.fetch():
        tags = read.get_tags(with_value_type=True)
        read.set_tags(tags)

I've tried earlier version (back to 0.8.4) and none of these are able to handle this sort of situation.

[example.zip](https://github.com/pysam-developers/pysam/files/777372/example.zip)

issue_title: Boolean envvars not being coerced
issue_body:
I believe that the boolean environment variables are not being correctly type coerced.

For example, in `disable_action`:
https://github.com/elastic/curator/blob/c8b8cb22618bb9836ed556062274c601406171e2/curator/validators/options.py#L30

If I use an envvar set as `"true"` or `"false"` (or `"True"` or `"False"`), it will say that it requires an int.
If I use an envvar set as `"0"` or `"1"`, it will also say that it requires an int.

I think that this is a problem with the `Coerce(bool)` validator, but I'm not completely sure (I never used Voluptuous).

Nevertheless, Voluptuous supports a [Boolean](https://alecthomas.github.io/voluptuous/docs/_build/html/voluptuous.html?highlight=coerce#voluptuous.validators.Boolean) validator. Maybe it would be better to use that one.

issue_title: list index out of range on multithreaded environment
issue_body:
Hi,
first of all, thank you very much for elasticsearch, you are doing a very nice job.
This is my first contribution to the community so please be nice with me.

I'm working on a multithreaded enviroment (using multiprocessing.pool.ThreadPool), and sometimes the following error appear:

```
File "/opt/clustering/wrappers/DAOElasticSearch/DAOElasticSearch.py", line 77, in indexDocument
    self.index(index = self.indexName, doc_type = self.docType, body = document, **kwargs)
File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/elasticsearch/client/utils.py", line 69, in _wrapped
    return func(*args, params=params, **kwargs)
File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/elasticsearch/client/__init__.py", line 261, in index
    _make_path(index, doc_type, id), params=params, body=body)
File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/elasticsearch/transport.py", line 326, in perform_request
    connection = self.get_connection()
File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/elasticsearch/transport.py", line 182, in get_connection
    return self.connection_pool.get_connection()
File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/elasticsearch/connection_pool.py", line 225, in get_connection
    return self.selector.select(self.connections)
File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/elasticsearch/connection_pool.py", line 66, in select
    return connections[self.rr]
```

Is this a problem with my code or is this a bug of elasticsearch client?
Looking over `/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/elasticsearch/connection_pool.py`, I think that this can be solved using some kind of Lock() or Semaphore() protecting `connections` variable.

Thanks again,
Victor.

issue_title: SLES 12 SP1 - error : no module named azure.common
issue_body:
Hi,

issue_title: Cloud config file not updated on CLI install update
issue_body:
Hi there,

We had a customer encounter an error where the recently added 'batch_resource_id' was not set in their cached clouds.config file, yet the Batch command module requested it. In other words - the cached config was out-of-date with the rest of the CLI installation, and didn't contain the latest fields as required by the modules. 
Is this expected behavior? Or should the CLI core update the cached config file between version updates?

The customer is running on Ubuntu 16.04 LTS, and their CLI install history is as follows:
- Initially installed the apt-get package from a few months ago, however this didn't work with [this tutorial](https://na01.safelinks.protection.outlook.com/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fdocs.microsoft.com%2Fen-us%2Fazure%2Fvirtual-machines%2Fvirtual-machines-linux-capture-image%3Ftoc%3D%252fazure%252fvirtual-machines%252flinux%252ftoc.json&data=02%7C01%7CAnna.Tisch%40microsoft.com%7C62e25bbad1954ed12b6a08d4867c326f%7C72f988bf86f141af91ab2d7cd011db47%7C1%7C0%7C636281315363138094&sdata=pZ%2F0SnHJzNpSrsq5jXcKmdBZ47JVKPEiP%2B4b8A%2BU4UE%3D&reserved=0)

issue_title: btMultiBody depends on bullet3 in debug version
issue_body:
The debug version of bullet 2.x causes a linker error unless bullet3 is also linked in. Unlike in release versions, the "if (0)" clause isn't optimized out at https://github.com/bulletphysics/bullet3/blob/8e163c984ddd0227ae8d901ea3a1a2b99ed1b2fc/src/BulletDynamics/Featherstone/btMultiBody.cpp#L850 and so b3Printf is still required.

You could use "#if 0" and "#endif" to comment out the b3Printf statement if that's the intention.

issue_title: async_q.get() can block the event loop under high load
issue_body:
When adding a lot of things to the queue async get will block for some time. Adding a sleep(0.001) helps, but will decrease throughput substantially. Especially with multiple threads adding to the queue.

```python
import asyncio
import time
import janus


async def get(q):
    while True:
        await q.get()


def add(q):
    while True:
        time.sleep(0.5)
        for i in range(0, 500):
            q.put(i)


loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
queue = janus.Queue(maxsize=100, loop=loop)
loop.set_debug(True)
loop.run_in_executor(None, add, queue.sync_q)
loop.run_until_complete(get(queue.async_q))
```
```
Executing <Handle <TaskWakeupMethWrapper object at 0x7f9f377b3078>(<Future finis...events.py:275>) created at /usr/lib64/python3.6/asyncio/locks.py:383> took 0.250 seconds

issue_title: Feature Request: Per message deflate configurable per message
issue_body:
## Long story short

It is called "per message compression" but is not configurable per message.

Suggested API change:

`coroutine send_str(data, compress=None)`

The `compress` argument allows to overwrite the value from `WebSocketResponse` on a per message basis.

Same for `send_bytes` and `send_json`

issue_title: jobs_directory breaks version checking
issue_body:
The Galaxy Pulsar runner has a version check that ensures the remote Pulsar is running a supported version. This is done by checking the value of `pulsar_version` in the job config dict returned by the job setup call in the Pulsar client.

With `jobs_directory` set, this remote Pulsar query is short circuited and the job config is created on the Galaxy side. In this case `pulsar_version` is set to the version of the Pulsar client library in Galaxy, so the check is comparing against the wrong version.

issue_title: Fallback to "Microsoft Visual C++ Compiler for Python 2.7" if "Visual Studio 9 2008 Win64" is not available.
issue_body:
By default, if the generator `Visual Studio 9 2008 Win64` is not available, scikit-build should try to set the environment associated with "Microsoft Visual C++ Compiler for Python 2.7" and the use `NMake Makefiles` (`Ninja` generated as suggested by @thewtex [here](https://github.com/scikit-build/scikit-build/issues/34#issuecomment-236369712)).

Example of use of `NMake Makefiles` with "Microsoft Visual C++ Compiler for Python 2.7" is presented in https://github.com/scikit-build/scikit-build/issues/34#issuecomment-230070388

Let's also note that since "Microsoft Visual C++ Compiler for Python 2.7" does *NOT* provide `devenv.exe` or `msbuild.exe`, it is not possible to use CMake generator like "Visual Studio 9 2008 Win64". This is discussed in https://github.com/scikit-build/scikit-build/issues/34#issuecomment-230541130

issue_title: Cache should be specific to an instance & small app
Removing state should not reschedule all handlers
issue_body:
From the docs: "removing a state causes the queue to be immediately re-evaluated. This ensures that a handler is never invoked when the state is no longer active"

Rather than tossing out the current queue of handlers and re-evaluating it completely, it would be better if the queue was scanned and handlers that should no longer trigger removed.

```python
def add_source(source, key):
    fetch.add_source(source, key)
    reactive.set_state('apt.needs_update')

@reactive.when('apt.needs_update')
def update():
    fetch.apt_update()
    reactive.remove_state('apt.needs_update')
```

In the above example, multiple handlers in the current iteration can call add_source. The slow update handler will not be called until after the current iteration of handlers has been exhausted and a new generation scheduled. Unless one of the handlers calls reactive.remove_state, causing the queue to be immediately reevaluated and this optimization lost.

Or is what I suggest already how it is implemented, or causes some other problem?

issue_title: only_once will be called multiple times
issue_body:
The guard used to stop only_once decorated code from running more than once includes the code line number in its key. The method will be run again if it is moved around the source file, or into a different source file for that matter. Which is fine, as I can argue that running only_once decorated methods  once after every upgrade-charm hook is a good thing.

I think this should be explicit, rather than random, and the was_invoked/marked_invoked caches cleared at the beginning of an upgrade-charm hook. If people do need a only_once_really decorator, the developer would need to provide a key rather than a volatile one generated for them.

issue_title: Explain how to build the DLLs from source
issue_body:
Thank you for providing these DLLs.

Would it be possible to provide additional information on how to re-create them from the Qt5 community source code?

More precisely, I faced this problem when trying to compile them myself: Python2.7 is requiring a build with Visual C++ 9.0 (as stated in [the WindowsCompilers Python wiki page](https://wiki.python.org/moin/WindowsCompilers#Which_Microsoft_Visual_C.2B-.2B-_compiler_to_use_with_a_specific_Python_version_.3F)), and Qt5 uses the C++ 2010 syntax which is not supported by Visual C++ 9.0.

What magic did you use to bypass this contradiction? Would it be a good idea to add this information in the README?

issue_title: Error with "-p no:terminal"
issue_body:
I have pytest.ini with `addopts = --instafail`.
I have other python program which must collect list of tests. Simple solution [here](https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/2039).
To disable pytest output I use argument `-p no:terminal` as suggested above.
The result is `AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'config'`
This happens because there is no terminalreporter plugin and `None` is returned here https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest-instafail/blob/55bb4d392867e3270a27dd0e6faa0c83441284a2/pytest_instafail.py#L32

issue_title: Make sure faulthandler displays crashes even after pytest is shutting down
issue_body: Crashes might happen during garbage collection of objects, well past the point `pytest_unconfigure` was called and pytest has even returned control to `__main__`.

issue_title: running the script on a different machine than the target webserver.
issue_body:
Goals:

1. I don't want to run the script as root (see issue #83)
1. I don't mind running commands as root on the target machine (see [`fabric`](http://www.fabfile.org/))
1. potentially multiple servers are involved (mass configure: puppet, chef, ansible, salt)

issue_title: Discontinue pycrypto support
issue_body:
*pycrypto* which we provided as crypto backend alongside *pyca/cryptography*  is no longer maintained ([tip of *master* 3 years old](https://github.com/Legrandin/pycryptodome/commits/master) and [usage discouraged in issue tracker](https://github.com/dlitz/pycrypto/issues/238)).

We should probably replace it with the drop-in replacement [*pycryptodome*](https://github.com/Legrandin/pycryptodome) or drop it altogether?

issue_title: `dateutil.parse.parse()` rejects `bytearray` as input
issue_body:
```
>>> import dateutil.parser
>>> dateutil.parser.parse(b'17 Jan 2017')
datetime.datetime(2017, 1, 17, 0, 0)
>>> dateutil.parser.parse(bytearray(b'17 Jan 2017'))
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/site-packages/dateutil/parser.py", line 1168, in parse
    return DEFAULTPARSER.parse(timestr, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/site-packages/dateutil/parser.py", line 556, in parse
    res, skipped_tokens = self._parse(timestr, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/site-packages/dateutil/parser.py", line 675, in _parse
    l = _timelex.split(timestr)         # Splits the timestr into tokens
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/site-packages/dateutil/parser.py", line 192, in split
    return list(cls(s))
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/site-packages/dateutil/parser.py", line 61, in __init__
    '{itype}'.format(itype=instream.__class__.__name__))
TypeError: Parser must be a string or character stream, not bytearray
```

Any function which can operate on a `bytes` ought to be able to operate on a `bytearray`---unless something has specifically been done to prevent that, as in `dateutil.parser.parse()`. When `bytes` works, it's unexpected that `bytearray` would fail. It would be better to accept `bytearray`s as well.

issue_title: utilities.py/_isnan(values) raised TypeError
issue_body:
#### _isnan return TyepError
When I tried follows codes, TypeError occured.

```python
map = folium.Map(location=[26.200627, 127.757604])
folium.PolyLine(locations=data[['latitude','longitude']].values).add_to(map)
# data is pandas DataFrame which has 'latitude' and 'logitude'
```
```error
TypeError: only length-1 arrays can be converted to Python scalars```
```
This error was caused by using math module in utilities.py/_isnan ()

``` python
def _isnan(values):
    """Check if there are NaNs values in the iterable."""
    return any(math.isnan(value) for value in _flatten(values))
```

So I suggest you modify it as follows
``` python
def _isnan(values):
    """Check if there are NaNs values in the iterable."""
    return np.isnan(values).any()
```

As all you know Python has builtin json support from 2.6 version (I think it is lower supported version of pyelasticsearch), so my question is why you place ``simplejson`` as library requirement?

    def default(self, value):

issue_title: Support for -`Name, -`Key`, and -`SearchKey` global attributes
issue_body:
This is a proposal to generalise the default schema we currently use internally.

Paging @slinnarsson, @gioelelm and @pl-ki for feedback. If everything is OK I can implement this, and Peter will be able to use it in his updates to the pipeline.

### Support for global attributes `..Name`, `..Key`, and `..SearchKey`

Currently, we use `Accession` as the "row key attribute", `Gene` as the default "row search key" attribute, and `CellID` as both "column key" attribute and "column search key" attribute. None of this is written down in the documentation.

It also ties the structure of our loom files to the current pipeline. Perhaps these labels will change in the future - they have already changed a few times internally in the past! 

Suggestion: add global attributes that state which of the row/column attributes are the key attributes. That way, writing scripts for the pipeline will be more "future proof", since it won't have to worry about changing labels. 

Similarly, we assume rows represents genes, and columns represents cells, but people might use loom for different purposes. I suggest adding a name attribute for that.

For backwards compatibility, we can use the above defaults as fall-backs if these attributes are missing.

The proposed schema ends up as:

- `rowName` (optional, is used to label the scatterplot view in `loom-viewer`. Defaults to "Genes" if `Gene` is present as a row attribute, "Rows" otherwise)
- `rowKey` (optional, _must_ contain a unique value for each row. Defaults to `Accession` if it is present in row attributes, uses row index numbers otherwise)
- `rowSearchKey` (optional, defaults to `Gene` if it is present as a row attributes, uses row index numbers otherwise)

- `colName` (optional, is used to label the scatterplot view in `loom-viewer`. Defaults to "Cells" if `CellID` is present in column attributes, "Columns" otherwise)
- `colKey` (optional, _must_ contain a unique value for each column. Defaults to `CellID` if it is present in column attributes, uses column index numbers otherwise)
- `colSearchkey` (optional, defaults to `CellID` if it is present in column attributes, uses column index numbers otherwise)

### Using `rowSearchKey` and `colSearchKey` for row/column getter functions

Currently, the documentation suggests the following way to access a row/column by Gene or CellID:

```python
>>> ds[np.logical_or(ds.Gene == "Actb", ds.Gene == "Gapdh"),:]
array([[  2.,   9.,   9., ...,   0.,  14.,   0.],
       [  0.,   1.,   4., ...,   0.,  14.,   3.]], dtype=float32)

>>> ds[:, ds.CellID == "AAACATACATTCTC-1"]
array([[ 0.],
       [ 0.],
       [ 0.],
       ...,
       [ 0.],
       [ 0.],
       [ 0.]], dtype=float32)
```

I think it would be convenient to create a helper function that would wrap this NumPy logic:

```python
>>> ds.getRows(["Actb", "Gapdh"])
array([[  2.,   9.,   9., ...,   0.,  14.,   0.],
       [  0.,   1.,   4., ...,   0.,  14.,   3.]], dtype=float32)

>>> ds.getColumns(["AAACATACATTCTC-1"])
array([[ 0.],
       [ 0.],
       [ 0.],
       ...,
       [ 0.],
       [ 0.],
       [ 0.]], dtype=float32)
```

These would use the `rowSearchKey` and `colSearchKey` attributes for choosing a default attribute, with the option for users to override this: `ds.getRows(list, searchKey=self.rowSearchKey)`

TODO list if this is all approved:

- [ ] add `rowName`, `rowKey`, `rowSearchKey`, `colName`, `colKey` and `colSearchKey` support to loom creation methods
  - [ ] `create`
  - [ ] `create_from_cellranger`
  - [ ] `_create_sparse`
- [ ] add `getRows` method to `LoomConnection`
- [ ] add `getColumns` method to `LoomConnection`

issue_title: HTTPTransport ignores credentials
issue_body:
Using coreapi-cli, JWT authentication failed for me:

```$ coreapi credentials add localhost "JWT `curl -s -d "username=user&password=pass" http://localhost:8000/jwt/ | cut -d\\\" -f 4`"
$ coreapi get --debug http://localhost:8000/schema
> GET /schema HTTP/1.1
> Accept-Encoding: gzip, deflate
> Connection: keep-alive
> Accept: application/coreapi+json, application/vnd.coreapi+json, */*
> Host: localhost
> User-Agent: coreapi
< 301 Moved Permanently
< Content-Length: 0
< Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8
< Date: Fri, 05 Jan 2018 14:54:47 GMT
< Location: /schema/
< Server: WSGIServer/0.2 CPython/3.5.2

....
```
No ```Authorization``` header was sent.

I figured out this line was incorrect:

https://github.com/core-api/python-client/blob/master/coreapi/transports/http.py#L352

Changing it to ```session.auth = DomainCredentials(credentials)``` fixed it for me.

issue_title: timeout message inconsistency
issue_body:
I'm curious why the Python and the SMTLib interfaces react differently to timeout commands.

For this script:


```python
from z3 import *
s = Solver()
s.set(timeout=1)
x, y = Ints('x y')
s.add(x**y == x)
result = s.check()
print result
print s.reason_unknown()
```

Z3 nicely says:

```
unknown
timeout
```

I'd consider the following to be the rendering of the same in SMTLib:

```
(set-option :timeout 1)
(declare-fun x () Int)
(declare-fun y () Int)
(assert (= (^ x y) x))
(check-sat)
(get-info :reason-unknown)
```

For this, z3 says:

```
unknown
(:reason-unknown "canceled")
```

The python response is much more accurate; as `canceled` is quite vague.

Would it be possible to have the unknown response be the same in SMTLib ? That is:

```
(:reason-unknown "timeout")
```

issue_title: IAR - Need to add a template for .ewd file
issue_body:
The debugger setting file is .ewd for the IAR IDE.
The .ewd file is not a template, but generated by a project generator regardless target and no way to configured.
The generated .ewd file is for cortex-M, and is not suitable for GR-PEACH (Cortex-A9) debug setting.
Therefore, users of GR-PEACH have to modify CMSIS-DAP section for debugger manually.
To configure .ewd file, please add a template for .ewd file.

We want to modify following:
- value of "state" in "CMSISDAPResetList"
- value of "state" in "CMSISDAPInterfaceRadio"

The former specifies the reset method. The value "10" is not valid to cortex-A9. Threfore IDE choose the default setting.
The latter specifies the interface is whether JTAG or SWD. I think that some platforms need to modify this setting.

issue_title: The caller object eats much memory
mipsel register-temp clash
issue_body:
Here's part of some translation:
```
------ IMark(0x4e47e4, 4, 0) ------ ; 0x4e47e4: move    $s0, $v0 (size = 4)
t1 = GET:I32(r2)
PUT(r16) = t1
------ IMark(0x4e47e8, 4, 0) ------ ; 0x4e47e8: lui     $t1, 0x19 (size = 4)
PUT(t1) = 0x00190000
```
The first instruction creates a temporary named `t1` (as the normal convention for pyvex), but things get iffy in the second instruction, where `t1` is actually a mipsel register.

It could be that the code generated is still valid, but i was relying on a separation between VEX temporaries and registers. Moreover, this just makes me feel bad, and i'm guessing you too?

        <error-info>
            <bad-element>target</bad-element>
        </error-info>
        <error-message>
            Missing element in the specified context - target datastore missing
        </error-message>
    </rpc-error>
</rpc-reply>
ncclient.operations.rpc.RPCError:
            Missing element in the specified context - target datastore missing
```

From Nokia documentation:
**The <copy-config> operation uses the CLI Content Layer format. The format of the source and target is block CLI.**

I'm _suspecting_ that the Nokia device is expecting a format like

<source><running/></source>
<target><startup/></target>

issue_title: VehicleMode should be an enum
issue_body:
VehicleMode should be an enum or at least enum-like. The biggest issue with the current implementation is:
```python
VehicleMode("GUIDED") == VehicleMode("GUIDED") is False
```
This means that determining which mode you are in either requires you to make your own enum:
```python
GUIDED = VehicleMode("GUIDED")
LAND = VehicleMode("LAND")
```
and only use those variables. 

Test for string equality:
```python
str(vehicle.mode) == "VehicleMode:GUIDED"`
````
Or access the `name` field (which is not documented):
```python
vehicle.mode.name == "GUIDED"
```

Additionally, being an enum would allow for code that checks whether a given mode exists programmatically:
```python
if "LAND" not in VehicleMode:
    # do something
```
and to iterate through the available enums:
```python
for mode in VehicleMode:
    menu.add(mode)
```

The type of enum I have in mind is detailed in [this StackOverflow post](http://stackoverflow.com/questions/36932/how-can-i-represent-an-enum-in-python)

issue_body:
```

HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Access-Control-Expose-Headers: Expires, Content-Length, Pragma, Backoff, Cache-Control, Alert, Retry-After, ETag, Last-Modified
Cache-Control: no-cache, no-store
Content-Length: 182
Content-Type: application/json
Date: Fri, 18 Aug 2017 16:52:04 GMT
Etag: "1503075082664"
Last-Modified: Fri, 18 Aug 2017 16:51:22 GMT
Server: waitress
X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff

{
    "data": {
        "a": "whoo",
        "id": "cc",
        "last_modified": 1503075082664
    },
    "permissions": {
        "read": [
            "hi"
        ],
        "write": [
            "basicauth:08eee2542a2328390dfaa7390fa7df0d64a7081b44d629bfacd9b4e02bd2676d"
        ]
    }
```

Let's get rid of the `read` permissions.

```
$ http -a 'user:pass'  PATCH http://localhost:8888/v1/buckets/b4/collections/cc "Content-Type:application/merge-patch+json" data:='{"a": null}' permissions:='{"read": null}'
HTTP/1.1 400 Bad Request

{

        {

        }
    ],
    "errno": 107,
    "error": "Invalid parameters",
    "message": "permissions.read in body: \"None\" is not iterable"
}
```

Note that JSON Patch does work by applying changes uniformly to `data` and `permissions`.

issue_title: REED behavior on migration
issue_body: In current implementation of OpenThread a REED drops its parent when he hears a MLE Advertisement from higher priority partition, and needs to reattach if the migration fails. According to the spec, it shall only leave it's current partition, after it succeeded in migration. Do we find this as an issue? I can look into it if we agree that it shall be fixed.

issue_title: Refactor `ratio`
issue_body:
`ratio` is still a bit of a weird parameters. Here is a proposal for further development:

* remove the deprecation regarding `float` as `ratio` which is a valid parameter with `binary` target.
* `'all'` in over-sampling should be changed to `'not majority'`. This is currently doing the same process but this is weird for the user.
* cleaning methods should have their own `ratio` type. `str` should be accepted but `dict` should be replaced by a `list` since we cannot select how many sample to select. Also `float` would not be accepted for the exact same reason.

`ratio` should also be renamed. My proposal `sampling_target`?

issue_title: Question about returning unique pointer in virtual function
Crashes when binding none to unsigned*
Problem with shared_ptr<T const>
issue_body:
Hello, first I want to thank the author(s) for working on this neat library. When it works for me, it's quite magical.

I'm working on a pybind11 module here:
https://github.com/cjdrake/boolexpr/blob/pybind11/python/boolexprmodule.cc

When I try to compile with GCC 5.3.1, I get the following error:
```
boolexprmodule.cc:63:77:   required from here
../third_party/pybind11/include/pybind11/pybind11.h:867:13: error: no matching function for call to 'std::shared_ptr<const boolexpr::Atom>::shared_ptr(std::shared_ptr<boolexpr::BoolExpr>)'
             new (&inst->holder) holder_type(inst->value->shared_from_this());
             ^
```

The reason I suspect there's a bug is that it can't find a matching function call for `std::shared_ptr<boolexpr::BoolExpr>`. But I'm not using that holder anywhere in my code or the pybind11 module. I'm using `std::shared_ptr<boolexpr::BoolExpr const>`.

The compiler error only happens when I uncomment line 63, the `Atom` class that is derived from `BoolExpr`. I'm hoping I just made some simple mistake, and you can help me with the correct documentation, but I just can't make it work.

issue_title: Support PetSet
issue_body:
Now we are using `PetSet` to deploy our cluster applications. We accept request in `Django` and it would be better to access `Kubernetes` with Python SDK like `pykube`.

After reading https://github.com/kelproject/pykube/blob/99e193a81f59044e1d6f3a3b96e6e33853059749/pykube/__init__.py , I thinks `pykube` doesn't support `PetSet` yet. Is it possible to support `PetSet`? 

We're also glad to test it.

issue_title: Ability to provide version-info.txt as environment variable?
issue_body: It's useful to have the COMMIT_ID available to our application, and the best way for us to provide it is as an environment variable. But talisker reads it from the `version-info.txt` file instead. Would it be possible for talisker to pick it up from *either* the file *or* an environment variable? Just to save us having to [export it in 2 different ways](https://github.com/canonical-websites/snapcraft.io/blob/master/Dockerfile#L15).

issue_body:
it would be nice to let vdirsyncer automatically set the date of an .ics file to the LAST-MODIFIED date of the event. that way you could easily see which events updated recently. in case this field is missing, DTSTAMP may be used.

i understand that i could implement a workaround by using hooks.

issue_title: standalone: add configurable wait before running challenges
issue_body:
In a setup with a reverse proxy sending the challenges to the standalone webserver, the challenges can fail because the reverse proxy may require a few seconds to resolve the standalone webserver and start redirecting traffic to it. As a result, this sometimes causes the challenges to fail.

Adding a (configurable) wait between starting the standalone webserver and running the challenges would solve this issue.

issue_title: Clean up spurious `try`/`except` statements
allow for more than one task of a given type in `upstreamArtifacts`
issue_body: Currently we key off of `task.name` in `find_task_dependencies`, which means more than one task of a given type will conflict.  It might be better to key off of `taskId` instead, which will still make the task unique, but will allow for multiple deps of the same type.

issue_title: Better define semantics and improve NettingChannelLibrary
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

At the moment reading the NettingChannelLibrary code can be confusing. Our smart contracts and especially the [NettingChannel](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/dbe4e4bd9e919d1bfffc12e4d0480aac9a987467/raiden/smart_contracts/NettingChannelLibrary.sol) lie at the heart of our system so they should be optimal and very easy to understand.

## Solution

Go through the code of the contract and make sure that all attributes and struct members are commented with a succinct explanation of what each one represents.

Additionally better define the structs that compose the channel. For example the [Participant](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/dbe4e4bd9e919d1bfffc12e4d0480aac9a987467/raiden/smart_contracts/NettingChannelLibrary.sol#L13) struct contains a lot of data that would better belong to a struct for holding `last_message_data`.

Moreover there are struct members like [`netted`](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/dbe4e4bd9e919d1bfffc12e4d0480aac9a987467/raiden/smart_contracts/NettingChannelLibrary.sol#L361) that we have as part of the participant but are only used at the settlement stage and as such don't need to exist during the entire life of a channel as they take up storage.

If there are other things that we can do to improve on readability, semantics and storage requirements of the contract please add them to this issue.

## Tasklist

- [x] Add comments to all attributes in the NettingChannel
- [x] Clearly separate the message data from the participant data. Use multiple structs.
- [x] Get rid of unneeded storage slots in an attempt to minimize storage needed for opening a channel.

issue_title: Make Ray API thread safe
issue_body:
The purpose of this issue to track the efforts of making Ray API fully thread safe.

# Existing problems
Although currently it's not allowed for users to call Ray API from non-main threads, there're also some internal background threads in the Python worker, which causes thread-safety issues.

1) One example is that the local scheduler client is used in 3 threads (main thread, import thread, and flush-profile-data thread). And the access of its underlying connection isn't always protected (see https://github.com/ray-project/ray/pull/2306#pullrequestreview-135707250 for more detailed explanation). 
2) Access of the buffer for profile data isn't protected.
3) worker.redis_client is also used in multiple threads. (Not sure if it's thread-safe, can anyone help confirm?)

# Todos
Besides fixing the existing problems, we'd also like to make Ray API fully thread-safe. It'll allow users to run multi-thread programs on ray. Here's the list of what needs to be done:
- [ ] Fix problem 1 by making the underlying c++ code thread-safe.
- [ ] Fix problem 2 by protecting the buffer with a lock
- [ ] Lock other clients and stateful operations in the worker (#2248)

# Java
TBD

# Related PRs
#2248
#2349

cc @alexbao

issue_title: Should we expose RunVars directly?
issue_body:
This is a pretty low-priority issue, but something to think about before 1.0 anyway:

Once #478 is merged, we'll have a few `RunVar` objects used directly in trio itself: the default thread limiter exposed via `trio.current_default_worker_thread_limiter`, and the hostname resolver and socket factory test hooks exposed via `trio.socket.set_custom_hostname_resolver` and `trio.socket.set_custom_socket_factory`.

For the last two in particular, the public functions are basically the simplest possible set/get APIs; they exist solely to hide the clunky old `RunLocal` API. Now we have `RunVar`, which is ... basically the same thing, a more usable API for setting/getting these kinds of variables. So maybe we should just... expose that? So e.g. `set_custom_hostname_resolver` would become `custom_hostname_resolver.set`?

The worker thread limiter is a little more subtle. Right now we don't expose any way to change the underlying default limiter object itself; you can adjust the number of simultaneous threads, by getting the object and mutating its `total_tokens` attribute, but you can't replace it wholesale with some other policy. Is there any use case for other policies? I guess you could make a C# thread pool style policy that admits threads after a delay that's proportional to the current pool size... probably anything fancy like this would be better handled by overriding the limiter for specific calls to `run_sync_in_worker_thread`, though. OTOH, exposing a `RunVar` isn't particularly problematic either. (I guess it would let you override the default with one that's just broken, but I don't think there is any particular danger here beyond any other sort of broken code.)

issue_title: MultiError.catch should take an exception type, like except: does
issue_body:
In regular Python, a catch-all exception handler looks like:

```python
try:
    ...
except Exception as exc:  # Don't catch KeyboardInterrupt, etc.
    logger.error(..., exc_info=exc)
```

In Trio we need to handle `MultiError`s, which might contain a mix of exceptions we want to catch and ones we don't, etc. So you have to use `MultiError.catch`, and the above becomes:

```python
def handler(exc):
    if isinstance(exc, Exception):
        logger.error(..., exc_info=exc)
        return None  # swallow this exception
    else:
        return exc  # let other exceptions propagate

with MultiError.catch(handler):
    ...
```

This is pointlessly cumbersome. We should make it look like this:

```python
def handler(exc):
    logger.error(..., exc_info=exc)

with MultiError.catch(Exception, handler):  # Note the extra argument
    ...
```

Hi,
there is an issue in dynamodb_listener.py", line 29, in forward_request related to JSON message encoding. It is exhibited by following crash while starting localstack:

user@machine:/tmp/localstack_install_dir$ make -C /tmp/localstack_install_dir infra
make: Entering directory '/tmp/localstack_install_dir'

2017-11-15T09:56:13:ERROR:localstack.services.generic_proxy: Error forwarding request: the JSON object must be str, not 'bytes' Traceback (most recent call last):

    data = json.loads(data)

    s.__class__.__name__))

My current workaround is to use older commits of localstack than the one I mentioned above.

issue_title: Can't edit projects
issue_body:
```
import gitlab

gl = gitlab.Gitlab('https://gitlab.example.com', 'hunter2', None, None, False)
project = gl.projects.get(12345)
project.save(wiki_enabled=False)
#or
project.wiki_enabled=False
project.save()
```

I get (on 657f0119a3 against Gitlab 9.3.9):

gitlab.exceptions.GitlabUpdateError: 400: {"error":"name, description, issues_enabled, merge_requests_enabled, wiki_enabled, builds_enabled, snippets_enabled, shared_runners_enabled, container_registry_enabled, lfs_enabled, visibility, public_builds, request_access_enabled, only_allow_merge_if_pipeline_succeeds, only_allow_merge_if_all_discussions_are_resolved, path, default_branch, repository_storage, approvals_before_merge are missing, at least one parameter must be provided"}

It looks to me like the API is passing a json object in the body of the PUT request, but gitlab, for some reason, wants ?wiki_enabled=False in the URL.  Or at least that's the only way I've managed to get this working with curl.

I'm not sure if this is your bug or gitlab's bug, but I'm filing it here because it seems like your code could be changed to make this work (and then I wouldn't have to wait until my gitlab admins upgrade before I can edit projects).

issue_title: Test parallelization garbles console output on Jenkins builds
issue_body:
Due to test parallelization, the build's console is written to by multiple processes concurrently and therefore has to be taken with a grain of salt. In particular, it could be that the console says

fooTest: FAILED
barTest: PASS

but that in reality fooTest passed and barTest failed.

The console output that is captured and then listed per test will be correct and can be trusted. Only the overall console output will be bad.

┆Issue is synchronized with this [JIRA Story](https://ucsc-cgl.atlassian.net/browse/TOIL-260)
┆Issue Number: TOIL-260

issue_title: 如何把多个图像放到一个echart生成的html里？
issue_body:
我有一个需求，是将公司云存储过去一周的三个数值（比较分别叫A，B，C）作图，但是c值很大，B和A的值是C的几乎是千分之一，所以做在一张柱状图里特别难看----高的高耸入云，低的鼠标都划不着。

所以我打算把C单独做一个图，B和A做到另外一个图里，用html展示出来。那请问如何在一个echart的python脚本里画两个图？我这个脚本应该如何改变？

现在脚本如下:

```
#!/usr/bin/env python
#coding=utf-8
from pyecharts import Bar

attr = ["{}day".format(i) for i in range(1, 8)]
attr = ["Mon", "Feb", "Wed", "Thu", "Fri", "Sat", "Sun"]    #横坐标
v1 = [1.49, 2.09, 4.03, 2.23, 5.26, 7.71, 7.56]
v2 = [0.3, 0.9, 0.2, 0.4, 0.7, 0.7, 0.6]
v3 = [758.15, 753.22, 801.28, 777.99, 828.7, 779.7, 775.6]    #值是随便写的，到时候会从redis里拿

bar = Bar("云存储情况总览", "本图表展示过去一周的云存储情况")
bar.add("A文件", attr, v1, mark_line=["average"], mark_point=["max", "min"])
bar.add("B文件", attr, v2, mark_line=["average"], mark_point=["max", "min"])
bar.add("C文件", attr, v3, mark_line=["average"], mark_point=["max", "min"])
bar.render('/root/test.html')    #生成一个html文件
```

issue_title: Signature Signing Incorrect?
issue_body:
I was testing my Java Port of the signature signing mechanism. I was using the Python version of the signature signing as a reference test. I could not get the Java Version of the signature to match the Python version. I also tried several online tools for signature signing and they would agree with the Java Version of my output and not the Python version. After a long head scratching, i think the bug is in Python version.

The python code in question in js.py

    key = hashlib.sha1(PRIVATE_KEY).digest()
    signer = hmac.new(key, message, hashlib.sha1)

shouldn't it be the following?

    signer = hmac.new(PRIVATE_KEY, message, hashlib.sha1)

The test case i was using is:

    PRIVATE_KEY = '0cc86846024a4c95a5dfd3111a532d13'
    js.sign_billing_info_update("ABC", 1312701386)

the message it would produce is :

    [1312701386,billinginfoupdate,[account_code:ABC]]

Output:
`'94c1af938a64b0d535dab615f7ba62cea9ffbc6f-1312701386'`

So the signature is
`'94c1af938a64b0d535dab615f7ba62cea9ffbc6f'`

And when using online tool or my Java Program is the following:
`'6afcdb714a0eae4d68048649f25677c3fa780257'`

Online Tool Used: 
http://hash.online-convert.com/sha1-generator

BTW, i was trying to replicate the signature generation example on the recurly site using the test key and test timestamp.
http://docs.recurly.com/recurlyjs/signatures/spec

-Talib
